The children are getting some rare free time. In this foothill village with only a couple dozen households, the children, whose parents are too busy to watch, are playing in the center of the village.
As you know, the Saintess has begun a Pilgrimage for our people. This village is her final stop before she ascends the mountain.
Remember what I told you, change into clean clothes, and do not disturb the Saintess when you see her. Am I clear?
Will the Saintess really be able to summon Kjeragandr back to us, Father?

My wife is working in the city, and I haven't heard from her since the disaster. People in the nearby villages say Sir Enciodes has put a blockade around the scene, and no one knows what is happening.
Do not worry, Bauer. This is exactly why the Saintess is embarking on her journey. Kjeragandr will not abandon any of Her children.
But all of us here in this village have already been abandoned, haven't we?
None of the young folk have been willing to stay on the farms since Karlan Trade started their business. Let me tell you, if Bauer didn't go lame when he was little...
Hey! Is this all you have to say, when the Father and I have been taking care of you since your son left for the city?
And I haven't heard a peep from him since the disaster. I'll be damned if I got anything else to say.
Alright, alright, let's not argue in the Saintess's presence.
Bauer, you should go check on old Carl. He said he wanted to catch a glimpse of the Saintess, even if he had to crawl on all fours. Help me talk him out of—
(Shouting) I see the Saintess, Father! She'll be here soon.
What? It's much sooner than I expected... Alright. Let's go wait by the roadside. And brighten your faces!
A few dozen villagers line the roadside, turning their faces to the distance, from whence the Saintess is approaching.

The cleric averts his eyes subconsciously, nervously rubbing his palms with his fingers. He was assigned to this village by the Vine-Bead Court twenty years ago, and has never seen the Saintess outside of portraits.
The footsteps approach. He focuses his entire attention on his facial muscles, hoping to leave a good impression on the Saintess.
Bam—
Oh—
(Whisper) Mali, you fool! Look what you've done!
What the... blast it, I forgot about you two.
A-Are you alright, Saintess?
Mali! Come and apologize to the Saintess.
I-I'm sorry, Saintess.
It's alright. Just a ball. It didn't hurt at all.
I still have to continue the ritual. Can you go home? Are your parents here?
S-Saintess—
Mom and Dad died a long time ago. My home is the temple.
I'll, er, go home then, Father?
(Under the breath) Go! I'll have you copying scriptures if you cause any more trouble!
*sob*—
I deeply apologize, Saintess...
How many children like him are there in the village?
I've taken in nine. Some are orphans, while others were entrusted to me by their parents who went to the cities to find work.
But we haven't heard from them since the disaster...
...Saintess! Do you know what's happening with this disaster? When can my wife come back?
What did I tell you, Bauer?
Has Kjeragandr really abandoned us?
Enough—
She would never do that.
In these days of hardship, we must remember, believe, and trust that She is kind and just.
I will do my best, so that your prayers, and the prayers of all Kjerag, will be heard by Her once again.
Please, trust me, in Kjeragandr's name.
By Kjeragandr, She will protect us, and protect the Saintess.
After a moment of silence, the villagers bow their heads together behind the cleric in a gesture of reverence. They have chosen to believe.
The Saintess nods and departs again. The snowy peaks in the distance await.
Madame Ratatos, I bring a message from my master regarding the meeting of the three clans a few days ago.
The Paleroches' stance towards the outsiders is firm. We can never be too careful regarding the faithless.
We must be even more vigilant now, with the Saintess out on pilgrimage and state affairs deferred to the three clans.
My master does not think ill of Sir Enciodes, but it is true that Clan Silverash has been too close to the Victorians. We do not know if he has other intentions.

My master has chosen to communicate with you, as he sees you as a woman who has always upheld Kjerag's traditions. He is happy to meet with you in person, if you have any thoughts you would like to share.
Are you finished?
Out of respect for Arctosz, please bring this message back to him.
I don't know if Arctosz is harboring a grudge because the Catastrophe occurred on Paleroche soil, and I don't care.
But at the last meeting, we reached the conclusion that none of us should make a move without direction from the Vine-Bead Court.
While the Saintess is absent, the best thing we can do for the nation is to take care of our own business and avoid causing more issues.
That will be all.
I see there is no avoiding these issues.
I apologize. But this little thing is no cause for concern.
Let us return to the topic at hand—long-term cooperation between premium Victorian clothing brands and Clan Browntail...
No, wait. With all due respect, Madam Browntail... I'm afraid this is not an issue I can ignore.
If the people of Kjerag are so hostile to Victoria, how are we to believe that any cooperation can last?
Our three clans may have our differences, but I am the sole representative of Clan Browntail. You may take my word on that.
This conflict between the three clans... I suppose that's not hard for a Victorian to understand. Not at all.
I have made great efforts to learn about Kjerag during my time here. Your culture is exotic, and fascinating.
While all of Kjerag is united by your Kjeragandr faith, the same tenets seem to have had different influence on the three clans.
The brave Paleroches, the clever Silverashes... but you Browntails, I must apologize that I find it hard to summarize your virtues in a single word.
I suppose the way I would put it, is that Clan Browntail is noble and trustworthy.
My sympathies for the homework you have to do.
Knowledge is the foundation of cooperation.
Her Grace the Duke values cooperation with Kjerag highly, and she is looking for a reliable, proper representative of your land to work with.
A representative of Kjerag? Is Her Grace unsatisfied with Karlan Trade?
Mr. Silverash is a most astute businessman, but this is also his greatest flaw. He will not permit any other winner in his game.
I'm sure you understand, as his neighbor.
Hmph... a keen summary.
Therein lies the problem. The three clans may equally be Kjeragandr's children in name, but in the eye of an onlooker like me, they are not.
Even an outsider like me can tell that your clan and the Paleroches have had virtually no effective role in response to this Catastrophe.
The Silverashes and Karlan Trade have not only monopolized Kjerag's foreign commerce, but also all of her foreign affairs.
And your nominal head of state, the Saintess—Mr. Silverash's sister, seems to have no objection to the present situation.
Mind your words, sir. Do not presume to blaspheme the Saintess's piety with your prejudice.
Oh, I apologize if my frankness has offended you. Please understand that I am speaking out of good faith, and simply conveying what I have observed as an outsider.
Cut to the chase. What's your proposal?
A simple one. Victoria is seeking friendly, equal, and truly mutually beneficial cooperation.
Effective, long-term cooperation. Not one that only benefits one side, or limited by narrow-minded nationalism.
Her Grace thinks highly of Clan Browntail, and of you personally. My presence here is proof of that.
The opinions of foreigners is of no importance to Clan Browntail. Your promise is the very proof of the shifting nature of Victoria's position.
You are wrong if you think Clan Browntail is simply another Clan Silverash, but easier to manipulate.
No, of course not, Madam Ratatos.
The Empire of Victoria does not seek subservience or dependence. She seeks to do the right thing.
Clan Silverash is a window that is sometimes open, sometimes closed. Only those inside the room can truly know its contents.
Tell me, Madam Browntail. Are you not tired of the constant, meaningless tribal conflicts that haunt this land?
Or perhaps... you aspire to lead Kjerag into a new era yourself?
I think that will do for today.
Thank you for your visit. I fully understand your proposal, and I have a message for you to bring back to the Duke.
She does not yet have a sufficient understanding of Kjerag, but I sincerely hope she will one day find it.
Noted. Unfortunately, any commercial cooperation must remain on hold while political issues remain unresolved.
But we have plenty of patience for an important partner such as yourself.
We look forward to your answer.

lines.st: Dear Ensia, I hope this letter finds you well.
By the time you read this, you will have already learned of my decision.
Don't worry. Think of it as your sister going on a long trip. She'll be back before you know it.
I'm sorry that we'll have to put our plans for fondue in the Vine-Bead Court's kitchen on hold, but I promise I'll be good as my word once I get back.
Don't worry about Enciodes either. He has his plans, though he might be busy for a while. We'll count on you to take care of things at home, alright?
There are a lot of things going on. Stay safe and be especially careful when going out.
I hope I'll find you as cheerful as always when I return.
I'll pray to Kjeragandr for both of you.
P.S.: Remember to keep your appointments at Rhodes Island, follow your doctor's orders, and wear protective gear when climbing the mountains.

lines.x: A letter from Enya?
Yeah...
It's always like this.
Same for Enciodes. It's quiet enough at home to begin with, and ever since I came back from my trip, he's barely been around!
These are exceptional times. They're doing all they can to protect Kjerag.
All we can do is trust them and support them...
I know... I just wanted to blow off some steam.
They still treat me like a kid, never telling me anything. I don't even know what I'm worrying for.
I want to help them too...
Lady Ensia, Master Chester...
Is Sir Enciodes here?
He's probably at the office. Why?

A herald has arrived from the Vine-Bead Court. They said they're meeting with Clan Paleroche to discuss cooperation in the joint mining venture, and they want Sir Enciodes to submit the relevant documents early.
I have the documents... but submit them early? Is this a temporary notice?
The herald didn't explain...
Very well. Prepare the car, I'll go—
Before he can finish, Ensia grabs the documents and runs out the door.
You can stay home, Uncle Chester! Let me handle it!
Ensia! Come back!
Sir Enciodes said you are not to go outside without permission!
It's alright! Enciodes must have forgotten to bring these with him. I'll have a good laugh at him later!
Come on, let's go!
Oh dear...
I should let Sir Enciodes know...
A brown car is driving on the mountain path to the Vine-Bead Court. Sitting in the back seat, Arctosz rubs his neck.
*yawn*
Why call me over so early in the morning? Is the mine thing so urgent?
I hear Sir Enciodes often stays overnight at the company these days. An early-morning meeting would fit his recent routine.
Hmph—
It's not like there's much to discuss. I'm just waiting for the Silverash documents to sign off on.
Give me the minutes from last time, Gulo. I'll take another look.
Yes, sir.
Sitting in the passenger seat, Gulo takes the documents from the glove box and is about to hand them to Arctosz when the car is violently jolted.
Gah! What was that?!
F-Falling rocks! They almost hit us!
Falling rocks? Here?
The car is once again violently jolted before he could finish, as falling rubble slams against the trunk.
It's not safe inside! We need to get out, sir! The road ahead is blocked!
Fy faen!
The three of them abandon the car and run to the mountainside, but the amount of falling rubble is overwhelming.
Look out—
A rock falls towards Arctosz. Gulo pushes him out of the way, and is struck by the sharp edge of the rock on his forehead. His massive frame falls on the ground, bleeding.
Arctosz crawls towards Gulo and pulls him closer to the mountain, leaving a long trail of blood on the ground.
The blood is soon covered by the falling rocks.
Gulo! GULO!
The tranquility of the mountain is broken by the roaring sound of engines as the morning mist disperses. Tranquility is not what Degenbrecher seeks right now.
She pushes her bike to its limits. The scenery flies by swiftly, but she does not see what she is looking for.
The tracks are still visible on the road; the target was here not long ago.
There—!
Degenbrecher jumps off the bike and rounds a corner—
Two cars are burning on the road after a collision, sending rancid smoke skywards.
Degenbrecher pulls her weapon and creates a gust that extinguishes the fire, revealing the familiar silhouette of a Silverash car.
The door is easily cut open. There is no one inside. Degenbrecher's worst fears have not come true.
She hears the sound of rocks clashing from the direction of the cliff.
Degenbrecher looks down at the source of the sound, and finds the one she is looking for, despite the dirt and soot on her face.
Degenbrecher!
Hanging off the side of the cliff with a hook, Ensia looks up at Degenbrecher, unsure how to hide her embarrassment.
Heheh... uh... I guess I screwed up...
...'Her kingdom promises not tomorrow.'
'Nor was it built for yesterday.'
'Those who walk the present are closest to Her.'
'Her blessings are ever beneath the traveler's feet.'
The snowflakes stick to her fur collar. The lone traveler pulls her hood tighter and walks up the mountain, against the cold wind that blows through the forest.
Enya pulls her cloak tighter around herself, her steps slowed by falling branches mixed with snow.
The clerics often say that Kjeragandr tests Her people with wind and snow, but Enya knows 'She' would never torment her like this.
Kjarr...
The Saintess sees the smoke of a campfire deep in the woods. Perhaps there is a place to rest.
Enya walks towards it and finds the fire still burning, providing a rare source of warmth. She reaches out to it at once.
The hides hanging around shows that it belongs to a hunter. Short shrubs surround a lone wooden house, but the owner is nowhere to be seen.
Then, one of the shrubs rustles. The tip of a metal bolt protrudes from it, pointing towards the campfire—
Who's there?
Enya turns around quickly, but not quick enough to avoid the bolt that strikes her right leg, soaking her thick clothing with blood.
She raises her bell instinctively as she stumbles from the pain. The attacker has emerged from the bushes, aiming his next bolt at the Saintess's heart.
Whoosh—
The attacker falls as the bell rings, his body quickly covered in snow. Slowly, the small lump turns red. It was not the holy bell that did this.
Enya gets closer, and sees a crossbow bolt in the attacker's neck.
A rough hand, small but strong, grabs Enya's shoulders and pulls her to one side.
Another bolt cuts through the air, taking down another attacker, but more of them continue to emerge from the forest.
Holzchopf bandits! How dare you commit murder at MY camp while I'm away!
A young huntress, carrying a heavy crossbow that seems too big for her, stands in front of Enya. She smells of herbs and cloudbeast.
Stop daydreaming!
You're a Silverash... What are you doing here?
Never mind... Stay close to me!
This is...
My home.
Last I saw, they were going the opposite way. It's safe here. I'll wrap you up, and then you need to leave.
Thank you. I—
Don't talk and don't fidget, Silverash.
Your wounds aren't that bad. There was some distance, and your thick clothes absorbed some of the impact.
But the bolt has a hooked head. It'll hurt when I pull it out. I'll try to be steady.
The young huntress snaps the arrow shaft and grabs the remaining part. She glances at the wounded woman, who simply nods, showing no sign of fear.
The huntress takes a deep breath and slowly pulls the arrow. Even she shivers at the sensation of the hook grating against flesh.
She does her best to keep her hands steady, until the arrowhead is completely pulled out.
Phew—finally...
Thank you... very much...
I'm not done yet. Hang in there a little longer.
She takes out a packet of powdered medicine, lifts the edge of Enya's dress, and rubs it skillfully on the wound before bandaging it up.
You can yelp if it hurts. I won't laugh.
I don't want to make you more nervous.
It'll only hurt more if your hands are shaking.
Hmph.
...Huh?
A slender, snow-white cloudbeast pokes its head out of the huntress's hood, hanging onto her braids with its claws and poking its wet nose around Enya's fur collar.
Then, it reaches out to sniff at the stranger's scent.
Don't touch it. It doesn't like strangers.
Before the huntress can finish, Enya's finger has already reached the little cloudbeast's chin. Simply touching its soft fur seems to bring warmth to her heart, even in the bitter cold.
(Whisper) Get back in, Rift Cloudbeast...
You call it... the Rift Cloudbeast?
Come to think of it, I don't know your name yet, my hero.
Joanna.
I don't need to know your name, Silverash. Don't think for a moment that we're friends.
Why? You've been good to me.
And how have you people treated me?!
Hmph... forget it. Nothing to do with you, anyway.
By 'you people', do you mean Clan Silverash?
Or Enciodes...?
Not just him.
My dad says the Silverashes have been obsessed with blaspheming Kjeragandr ever since Enciodes's father was clan leader.
He poured asphalt on Kjeragandr's backbone, and poison on Kjeragandr's tail. His greed led to his downfall.
Enciodes hasn't learned his lesson. He's done even more to wreck Kjeragandr's peace.
But his greatest sin was the hole he dug in Kjeragandr's flesh. This disaster is proof that Silverash has angered Kjeragandr.
But Kjerag can't stay in the snowy mountains forever.
Haven't industry and trade brought us better lives? Why would the kind Kjeragandr punish us just because we're living better lives?
Better lives?

You Silverashes sit by the warm fireside, eating the best food and drinking the best wine...
But who has to pay the price?
Us, we do!
Many beasts lived at the foot of the mountains when I was little.
For a thousand years they lived off the land—drank from the rivers, and went back to the forest to find berries and leaves.
But Karlan Trade's railroads divided the mountains and rivers, forcing the beasts to risk their lives crossing the railway.
You even blame them when your trains run them over!
The beasts were forced to move higher, but there isn't enough room there for large packs. They had to fight each other for space and food, and their numbers dwindled.
Not to mention all the cloudbeasts that are sensitive to noise... you have no idea how many died, and how I ended up taking in my Rift Cloudbeast!
But the farmers by the mountains need the fertilizers and seeds that the trains bring. And sick people on the mountains need medicine.
Where's the bountiful game that Kjeragandr once gifted us, then?
The beasts grow fewer each year. Even water from the springs is less sweet.
My family made a living hunting in the mountains for generations, following the holy teachings of Kjeragandr, and we show our faith by sculpting statues in Her image.
Dad... myself, and my siblings, we've always prayed to Kjeragandr, always paid our respects.
Why are WE the ones who have to pay the price?!
Spooked by the sudden outburst, the Rift Cloudbeast leaps down from a trembling Joanna and goes over to Enya.
Enya bends down and lifts it up, placing it back on Joanna's shoulder.
Don't move, or your wounds will reopen.
This is the first time I've heard about any of that.
If you want to argue, go ahead, Silverash.
No, I don't.
I'm... different from the others. I have to stay at home most of the time, and what I know about the land today comes mostly from the accounts of others.
Sometimes I go out and experience things for myself, but it's not enough.
Kjeragandr will never abandon any children of Kjerag. I promise.
Hmph...
You better promise yourself that you pull through whatever's going on...
What was with those bandits, anyway? I know all the hunters living on the mountain. I didn't recognize any of those guys.
I think... they came for me.
For you? Do you have enemies?
Hmm... It's a long story.
If that's the case, you'd better stay put tonight. They won't give up so easily.
My dad's friend has a shop in town, and my siblings have been helping out these past few days. I'll go get some medicine from him.
And get help to fight the bandits.
No, don't.
Why? Are you afraid to be on your own? I'm fast and strong. I can be back by tomorrow morning if I go down the mountain now.
That's not what I'm worried about...
Why? You Silverashes are such a bother. If you have something to say, just say it.
If you can't fight, I have no choice but to get help from town. That's the deal.
Wait, I can tell you—
Enya hurriedly gets up, tugging at the sleeve of the huntress who is about to leave, causing her own cloak to fall off.
The S-Saintess...?
Actually, I'm...
I-I'm sorry!
Please, Your Holiness, punish me for my insolence!
No, I'm not going to punish you...
Please, just sit down...
Enya tries to stop the huntress, but she quickly takes a step back, putting more distance between herself and Enya.
She turns to leave, but then seems to remember something, and spins back around and bows reverentially to Enya, before finally leaving the room.
*sigh*...
General Gulo...
Could you knock on the cheibe door before barging in, Matterhorn boy?
What are you doing here?
I was accompanying Lady Ensia for a checkup. She suffered a car accident this morning, but fortunately she's unharmed.
I was told you were here too, so I thought I should pay a visit—are you alright?

Hmph, just a scratch. But—
Wait, you said something happened to the young Silverash lady?

Lady Ensia was delivering something to Sir Enciodes when she suffered a head-on collision with another vehicle on the way to the Vine-Bead Court. It was fortunate that Madam Degenbrecher quickly arrived on the scene.
You said she was going to the Vine-Bead Court too?
Strange. My master received a summons from the Vine-Bead Court this morning, saying there was a meeting, and it was on the way there that we were struck by falling rocks.
...Did you hear anything from the Browntails?
No... not yet, at least. What's the matter with the Browntails?
No wonder Sir Arctosz was fuming when he left... I haven't seen him so angry in a long time.
But this—argh!
Listen, brother, I need you to do me a favor. Can you ask Sir Enciodes to go to the Browntail manor at once?
Sir Arctosz is even more hot-headed than I am. I'm afraid he might do something impetuous. Sir Enciodes is the only one who can stop him.
I see... I'll let him know immediately.
I suggest you get your head checked at the hospital, Arctosz. The symptoms of a mild concussion are easy to miss.
Don't try to change the subject!
Answer me! Why is it that only Clan Silverash and mine were attacked, while you Browntails sit safe and sound in your home? What do you know?!
Everything that I know about what happened today came from you.
More excuses!
Even the servants of Silverash knew to show concern, and tell me that Ensia suffered too.
But what about you? You look like you couldn't care less, like it's nothing more than a piece of snow falling on the ground! Is this the Browntail stance?
I apologize for my demeanor. All of Kjerag has suffered in recent days. I ask for your understanding.
Enough of this hypocrisy.
We both know there's been gossip going around about the Browntail Witch.
I never took it seriously. I couldn't care less what you did, as long as you did it for Kjerag and Kjeragandr.
But now?
When did Kjeragandr teach you to stab your allies in the back, Ratatos?
Watch your words, Arctosz. Do not disrespect Kjeragandr, even if you intend to insult and provoke me.
Stop dodging the question and answer!
Was Clan Browntail involved in the attack on me and Ensia?
You're wasting your time. I've already told you multiple times: I know nothing about it, and I express my sympathies.
If that's your final answer, then don't accuse me of going too far.
What do you mean?
Don't think I'm out of Paleroches just because you took Gulo out of action. Before I went out, I told my men when to show.
Clan Browntail's estates will be thoroughly searched. I didn't want to go this far, but I'm afraid Sciurus and her children will have to bear with me a little.
You dare?!
You made me do this, Ratatos!
The door to the drawing room flies open without warning. Arctosz draws his sword immediately.
The sharp-eyed Browntail guards raise their weapons too and stand in front of Ratatos.
I take it you've already discussed the incidents that occurred today.
I came for the same reason, and I've reached certain conclusions. Please, stand down.
Seeing that Arctosz has sheathed his sword, Ratatos raises a hand, and her guards hesitantly back off.
Good timing, Enciodes.
With the leaders of Clans Paleroche and Silverash both present, we demand a thorough audit of Clan Browntail's documents, and cooperation from Clan Browntail in the investigation.
Sorry, Arctosz, but I don't assent to the demand.
What?
Why would Ratatos want to get rid of us?
Assassinating the heads of the other two clans at this moment would only cause even more chaos.
Clan Paleroche has the most warriors, while Clan Silverash runs Karlan Trade.
Ratatos could not hope to keep the situation under control if you and I both died in an accident. Meanwhile, retribution would be swift and harsh.
Am I right?
Ratatos avoids Enciodes's gaze and nods lightly.
That doesn't explain why Clan Browntail wasn't attacked.
Maybe a coincidence, maybe bad timing. Maybe the attackers had something planned for the Browntails too, and we just don't know about it.
You won't convince me with talk of coincidences, Enciodes.
Who would have done such a thing, if not Ratatos?
This is not a question we can solve in a short time.
Don't forget that there are plenty of people looking to take advantage of the chaos, and take a piece of Kjerag for themselves.
This assassination coincided with the arrival of Victorian aid. The mastermind wants to keep us from working together.
The Victorians did this?
They are potential suspects, as is any foreign politician who attempts to intervene at this juncture.
Therefore, I would like to maintain the status quo of our previous meeting—I will continue to handle all external affairs, including those involving Victoria.
I will discover the truth, for Clan Paleroche, for the peace of the three clans... and for Ensia.
I'll trust you one last time...
Hmph...
Don't you dare look smug, Ratatos. You got lucky today. You can thank Enciodes and Sciurus.
If it turns out you're behind all this after all, you'll get what's coming to you!
The sturdy Ursus slams the table, then turns and leaves.
Thank you for calming that maniac down, Enciodes.
But I'm exhausted. I'm sure you understand how tiring it is to deal with baseless accusations.
If you suspect me and want to question me, I—
There's no need for that. Trust between the three clans has never been more important than now.
I have other urgent matters to attend to. I'll take my leave.
Pardon me for not seeing you out.
Ensia wasn't hurt. You can rest easy.
I was the one they were after.
Obviously. The accident was very likely staged. They targeted your car, without knowing who was inside.
Something's not right... it's too forward. It's as though the people behind this want to make the point that they have no qualms about deploying extreme measures.
They're trying to sow chaos and suspicion. The act itself is more important than the result.
There is another possibility... perhaps it's not me, Ensia, or Arctosz whom they're truly after.
There will be trouble on the mountains.
It's Enya they're after?
If anything happens to the Saintess at this point, it will serve to affirm all the conspiracy theories, and drag Kjerag further into the quagmire.
I need you to protect Enya, Degenbrecher.
Enya set out a long time ago. I can't promise anything.
You need to prepare for the worst.
I believe in Enya.
You 'believe'.
And you will not provide any of your basis for that belief to reassure me? Like you usually do?
Correct.
I believe.
Arrggghhh! Didn't I tell you not to bother me today?
Who's—
...Sister.
What are you doing here...?
Don't just stand there, take a seat. What's the matter, can't sleep?
I thought I told Yucatan to keep a close eye on you—
No...
She shakes her head.
I told Yucatan not to come with me.
I wanted to talk to you, one-on-one.
Ratatos never thought she would see this expression on her sister, who is usually so cheerful and straightforward.
Alright. But only for a little bit. Then you go back to bed.
*sigh* Acting like a little girl, when you're supposed to be a mom already.
I heard Arctosz shouting in the drawing room today.

Damn, so he did disturb you?
I'll have his hairy head for this.
No, it's not that...
I heard what you were arguing about, and I know many bad things have been happening lately...
Please, Rata... if you do know something, tell me.
By Kjeragandr...
You shouldn't be worrying about these things right now, Sciurus.
You'll have plenty to worry about once you take my job.
But there's been trouble after trouble. Even I, shut up in my room, can tell that things are getting worse...
I see unhappy faces outside the window every day, not to mention we haven't heard anything back from Monch.
I have people searching for her... I'm sure she's fine. Hey, don't forget that she's one of the finest Browntail warriors!
I know...
It's you I'm really worried about, Rata.
I've never helped very much, but there was a time when I could at least stand by your side and give you my support...
But now, you're facing all this on your own... I wish you would talk to me more.
There's no need to carry everything on your shoulders. Yucatan and I have your back, no matter what.
You think too much, my silly little sister. Are you really in such a hurry to take over for me?
That's not what I—
I promise you, Sciurus.
I'll protect you, and Yucatan, and all of Clan Browntail.
If it's the last thing I do.
Kjeragandr be my witness.
Still don't believe me? But you've got to believe Kjeragandr, right?
There's no need to go that far...
Alright... then promise me you're going to bed now.
Tomorrow will be a better day.
You honor me with your invitation, Madam Browntail.
I take it your thinking has changed, then?
...Cut the crap.
Assassinating the members of Clan Silverash and Clan Paleroche, driving a rift between the three clans... is this how you intend to coerce me into working with you?
I thought Victorian politicians were smarter than that.
The man smiles nonchalantly.
I'm not sure how such a notion could have occurred to you, Madam Browntail.
Assassinating the leadership of another country on their own soil... it would be tantamount to a declaration of war... if it could be proven.
Are you sure you want to accuse Victoria of such crimes?
So this is why you people seem so smug about yourselves.
I'm not sure I understand your meaning.
But since you've deigned to meet with me today, I am very happy to listen—is there anything I can help you with?
Go on, act smug. Your crude little plot worked, huh? The relationship between the Paleroches and Browntails has broken down completely, and I've become the most suspicious one here.
As for Enciodes... he just spun his tale about all three clans wanting stability to hoodwink that fool Arctosz.
He knows full well that, in the present state of Kjerag, should the leader of any of the clans meet an 'accident', it would take little effort to demoralize the already-disheartened people.
If he learns of our meeting, he will not hesitate to join forces with the Paleroches to attack me, even if he knows full well I wouldn't promise you anything.
After all, what Kjerag needs right now is a culprit, one to place all the blame on.
In that event, it's entirely possible that Clan Browntail will be wiped off the map.
...I don't have much time left.
My sympathies, madam. I am in full agreement with your assessment of the situation.
Do you intend to turn things around, then?
Let's get straight to the point.
First, I want an absolute guarantee that you will prioritize the safety of Clan Browntail and the people of my domain, no matter what happens.
Of course. We have long sympathized with the ordinary folk who have become marginalized by Clan Silverash. It is one of our major goals for peaceful cooperation with Kjerag.
Ugh... say whatever you want.
Second, all the trade policies currently in place will remain so when Clan Browntail takes over from Karlan Trade, and Victoria must not withdraw any assistance currently offered to Kjerag.
Otherwise, we reserve the right to terminate our cooperation with immediate effect—not something you want to happen.
You need not worry, madam. We are more than eager to cooperate with Kjerag, and would never dream of undermining this partnership.
There will be much room for further cooperation, once you become the sole representative of your nation.
Good...
Taking over Karlan Trade isn't something that can be accomplished overnight. We need time to prepare... and the first order of business is to deal with Arctosz.

I know that man, and he will come looking for trouble if he finds out Clan Browntail has been working with Victoria.
In a sense, he is an even bigger problem than Enciodes...
That bloody fool!
Most regrettable...
The decision is yours, Madam Browntail. How should we... persuade Arctosz?
I will contact him again... for one final meeting.
Oh?
There is an old Browntail manor at the foot of the mountains in the southwest, an area that is virtually uninhabited.
I will send Arctosz a message, telling him to meet me there tomorrow, where I will explain the truth.
The time of the meeting is set at 7pm... fifteen hours from now.
I hope Victoria will demonstrate its good faith.
Of course. You will not be disappointed.
We look forward to your 'persuasion' of Clan Paleroche.
The mountain wind carries the snow, battering the hay-thatched roof.
A few dry leaves are blown into the air, landing on the braids of the girl sitting on the rooftop. She picks it out and rubs it in her fingers.
The Rift Cloudbeast pokes its head out from behind the girl's shoulders, rubbing against her red cheeks.
Rift Cloudbeast...
I'm just... clearing my head. You don't have to come out and be cold with me together.
The little white creature, which has endured many harsh days and nights with Joanna, purrs gently.
Thanks...
I... I don't know how to face the Saintess...
She's the vicar of Kjeragandr. I should love and honor her, but—
How could those things happen... Why must my family be punished...?
The girl sits quietly on the roof, Rift Cloudbeast in her arms, until the fine snowflakes cover her hair in white.
The door leading to the backyard is pushed open.
The visitor has added a few shallow footprints on the snow. Enya sees three scattered crossbows as she walks to her left, and picks up one to examine it.
It is smaller than the one that Joanna uses.
Enya casts her gaze in front, and there are three targets standing a short distance away, with a few bolts on them that have yet to be retrieved.
They started training at such a young age...
There is a name on the stock... does it belong to her siblings?
Enya's attention is drawn to a bunch of sculptures scattered haphazardly on the ground at the other end of the yard. Their visage is a familiar one.
Enya approaches the largest amongst them and holds it before her. The sculptor was obviously extremely skilled—Kjeragandr is smiling gently upon her.

This is...
just like the one we built at Lake Silberneherze.
I guess this is what they all think you look like.
There are other sculptures lying by her feet, and Enya picks up one of them.
This one is sleeker, the chisel marks on the face rougher, giving it a sense of harshness. Perhaps the sculptor had used too much force when sculpting the face, and chiseled off a little too much.
It's not right, is it? Doesn't look like Kjeragandr at all.
The face needs to be a little thinner, and the body...
Will you heed the plea of your humble handmaiden, Saintess?
Enya, pleeease—
See? It looks better when the face is rounder.
She carefully puts the two sculptures of different sizes back.
In this bare, unfamiliar backyard, Enya puts her hands together and prays toward the statue of Kjeragandr, as the elders of Vine-Bead Court often do.
She does not make any wish. She only hopes for a miracle.
The sun has set, but the huntress still sits in the yard.
She hurriedly gets up to bow when she sees Enya, but the latter lays a hand on her shoulder.
...Saintess.
No need for formalities. It's just us two.
I would prefer it if you could call me 'Enya'.
As you wish.
After greeting Enya, the girl looks aside and falls quiet again.
There were a lot of Kjeragandr sculptures in the backyard. Did you and your family make them yourselves?
(Nods in silence)
They look a little different. Is it because people interpret the image of Kjeragandr depicted in the old books differently?
My dad made the ones that looked like Her. I made the ones that don't...
Pfft—
I'm sorry! I just wanted to ask... why did you make all those sculptures?
My family is a branch of Clan Paleroche that went into the mountains on Kjeragandr's orders. We've been hunters ever since.
We believe all that lives in the mountains share Kjeragandr's soul, and we are all part of the eternal cycle of life within Her.
In gratitude for the blessings of Kjeragandr, every member of our family must learn to sculpt the image of Kjeragandr once we are old enough to hunt alone.
We also make leather jackets, bone sculptures and accessories with Her mark.
'Hear, blessed hunter.'
'Are the whispers of the wind and the grass not Her hymns? Are the creatures that walk and fly not She embodied?'
'The cycle of life and death is the cycle of water and ice; there is neither beginning nor end.'
'Speak of Kjeragandr's wisdom and love, and listen.'
'Rely not on signs and omens, but seek upon snow-covered peaks'... and then... and then—
'And become one with Her.'
Yes... this part feels different from the other parts of Kjeragandr. I always get stuck here.
The details of the scriptures are not what matters the most. What's important is to be constantly mindful that Kjeragandr watches and listens to our words and deeds.
You may not remember the complicated verses, but you're living, hunting, and sculpting in accordance with Her teachings.
Even as the Saintess... I may never have truly understood the meaning of this passage, if I hadn't met you.
You're one of the most pious faithful I've ever met. Kjeragandr will protect your family.
Really?
Yes. I'm Her... chosen Saintess, after all. She will appear when we need her most, no matter what hardship we face.
*sob*—
Tears roll down the young cheeks of the huntress who always puts on a strong front, falling and melting the snow by their feet.
Joanna? What's the matter? Can you tell me?
I, I—
Enya takes Joanna's trembling hands in hers.
When I first learned to hunt, I was bitten by an Infected beast... and I got Oripathy...
Dad hunted every day and made those sculptures after coming back from the hunt, so that he could sell them in town to buy medicine for me... and he did it for years...
A few days ago, dad's friend told me... he was at the station... the day the Catastrophe happened...
If I didn't—
Joanna...
Why, Kjeragandr...?
The grieving girl drops to the ground, curled up in a fetal position.
Enya kneels by her side, trying to find words of consolation, but for all the scriptures she has memorized, none have an answer. After a brief hesitation, she begins patting the girl's back.
There is a lump in her throat. If even eternal Kjeragandr can fall silent, what could Her Saintess possibly do?
Are you listening, Kjarr...?
Sitting by the window, the Rift Cloudbeast suddenly growls with as much volume as it can muster, and darts back into Joanna's hood.
Joanna sniffles and wipes her tears with her palms.
Someone's coming. I'll get the door.
Young lady...
How do I get to the nearest town?
Are you lost? Do you see those lights? Follow the trail down the mountain, and follow the lights whenever you come to a crossroads—
It's getting dark... and my eyesight is poor...

I'll walk with you—
No... I can't leave right now.
You could spend the night here, and head down once the sun rises.
No, no, I shouldn't trouble you. I'll find the path down the mountain...
What is it?
Just an old man who got lost.
You're quite far from town. Do people pass by here often?
No. My family are the only ones who live here. We don't see many passersby. In fact, you were the first...
—Look out!
Back already, Gulo? You still have the gauze on your head.
I told you to think before you act! You get back to the hospital right now and stay in bed.
Master... I heard that you're going to meet with Ratatos again, at the Browntails' invitation.
You can't go...
What, you think I'm afraid of her?
That witch says she's going to tell me the truth. I'm eager to see what she spews from that mouth of hers.
But master... the meeting is on Browntail turf. This is clearly a trap.
Hmph, you think I don't know?
A Paleroche never runs from a fight!
Just because it's her turf doesn't mean a Paleroche is helpless there!
You know what? Seeing that gauze on your head is making me doubly-mad!
I don't care how mad you are, master! I'm sitting right here at the door and not budging an inch until you change your mind!
And what does any of that have to do with you?
It's my own fault I got hurt and couldn't protect you. All I can do is stop you from putting yourself in danger.
Uh... I'm not angry at you! I'm angry at those bloody Browntails.
Don't worry, I'll settle the score for you. I'll show them nobody messes with Clan Paleroche.
Get out of the way, brother.
Master...
Warriors, take General Gulo back to his room.
Several warriors help Gulo up and help him walk back to his room.
Hmph.
I'm not afraid of them. But I won't go unprepared.
Fy faen. Prepare a few more cars, boys.
Dinner is ready, Madam Sciurus.
What about the matriarch? Will she not join us?
Matriarch Ratatos just left. She says not to worry about her.
I remember the matriarch saying she had a meeting with Clan Paleroche today. But it's so early...
Did she go alone?
We're not sure...
Something doesn't feel right, Yucatan.
Go after her.
Alright...
One moment, please, Madam Sciurus and Master Yucatan...
What is it this time?
The matriarch left orders before she left...
We are not to allow the two of you to leave home tonight.
Under any circumstances.
Saintess! Get down!
Drat... Who are these people?
How dare they attempt to murder the Saintess on Kjerag soil?!
Maybe they're not from Kjerag...
They're dangerous...
Don't worry, Saintess! I'll protect you with my life!
They won't get in so easily... Don't underestimate the hunters of Kjerag!
The young huntress locks and loads her heavy crossbow, barely taking any time to aim it through the broken window before pulling the trigger.
It is immediately followed by multiple screams.
Hit.
Careful, there are a lot of them!
We can't just stay here. We need to get out...
What do we do—
Calm down. Listen to me...
Lying prone on the snow, the attacker gestures to those behind him to remain hidden. They learned their lesson the hard way.
They slowly close in on the house, on guard against the crossbow bolts that can be fired from the window at any time with uncanny accuracy.
They could see a shadow move behind the curtain, and the edges of the curtain sway...
The attacker raises his crossbow.
The poisoned bolt strikes the figure behind the curtain. He hears the sound of the body hitting the floor, then silence.
Hit. Not sure if she's down.
Units Two, Three and Four, hold position. Unit One, with me. Approach with care. The intel says there are two of them.
Several figures sneak up to the house, breaking in once they have determined no sound coming from inside.
What...? This... It's...
...A wooden sculpture?
A sculpture of Kjeragandr lies on the ground, a crossbow bolt sticking out of its head.
The assailants did not notice the delicate sound of machinery operating as they entered the room.
They hear the ringing of a bell, while their arms and legs go weak.
Five loaded crossbows and a massive net take aim at the invaders.
They fell for it! Just like you said!
You'd better repent for damaging a sculpture of Kjeragandr!
Now! Run!
Alpha, report.
The Paleroche convoy has cleared the third checkpoint.
Confirm target position—back seat of the lead car.
Shall we engage?
Affirmative.
Wait, there's a change in the situation!
The target's stopped moving. No direct line-of-sight. I can't see what's going on.
Browntail warriors? What are they doing here?
Stand by. I'm calling Number One.
What's the meaning of this?
Your matriarch told us to come to meet her, then sent you to stop us?
The location of the meeting is just ahead, Sir Arctosz. This will only take a minute of your time.
Please allow us to search your vehicles, in the interest of safety.
What a joke. You're afraid of a handful of boys when your people picked the location? You people are cowards.
The orders came from Matriarch Ratatos. We request your cooperation.
Hmph. Do what you want. I've got nothing to hide.
No issues. You may proceed now, Sir Arctosz.
Not so fast.
You searched us. Now, we search you.
Search every nook and cranny from here to the Browntail manor, the road and everything around it.
You find anybody suspicious, take them in!
Yes, sir!
Report on the target's location, Alpha.
Come in, Alpha.
Alpha, can you hear me? Report!
What's going on...?
Command, the target car has entered our surveillance zone and is approaching the final point of action. Should we move?
When did he... is there any movement from the target?
No, the target hasn't exited the vehicle. I can't see inside from here.
Stand by. Engage once you have visual on the target.
Bravo reporting.
We have visual on what we believe to be an armed Kjerag patrol.
Have you identified them?
Unclear, possibly Paleroche guards.
Charlie reporting! We've spotted Kjerag warriors too. They're searching nearby buildings.
Our position may be compromised. Should we make a move now?
Negative, stand by.
We've been compromised! Engaging!
They found us! We've been exposed, repeat, we've been exposed!
Permission for final action!
Bloody hell...
Affirmative—
The north winds blow, over the mountain road...
The tuskbeast hides, among the rocks he bides...
Take your bow, swing it low...
...By the little wood window.
Hit.
The forest is the domain of the hunter.
The young huntress takes Enya into the forest, the mysterious assailants in hot pursuit.
Their numbers are soon thinned by crossbow bolts that seem to come out of nowhere.
There's at least one more... where are you...?
The huntress knows that her last shot has revealed her position, while the enemy has withdrawn into the shadows.
She hides behind a large tree, zeroing in on one little sound.
Lock, load, loose—
Missed?!
The young huntress is pushed to the ground as her shot misses.
The attacker picks up a bolt from the ground and aims it at her throat.
But he never gets the chance to bring his raised hand down. It is frozen.
A blurred silhouette appears from above the assailant, bringing the weight of a thousand mountains down upon his head.
The ground trembles, and the snow on the tree branches is scattered...
...Degenbrecher?
Degenbrecher catches the bolt flying towards her with one hand.
Who are you?! What do you want with the Saintess?!
Stop, Joanna! Degenbrecher is a friend.
Degenbrecher...?
Joanna hesitantly removes the bolt from her crossbow as she looks up and down at Degenbrecher.
I've heard your name before... they say you're a strong warrior.
But I've never seen... a man beaten into permafrost like that...
What are you doing here, Degenbrecher?
Enciodes sent me.
I apologize, but now is not the time to stick to the rules.
How did he know I was in danger...?
This is not the only attack in recent days.
There were attempts on the lives of the members of Clans Silverash and Paleroche, soon after you left the Vine-Bead Court. The three clans are on the brink of civil war.
How could this be...?
These people...
Only now do the trio have the time to take a long look at the assailants—late assailants, more precisely.
They are wearing Paleroche armor, and they are carrying standard-issue Kjerag weapons.
Clan Paleroche...?
That's impossible!
Clan Paleroche has always been loyal. They would never think of murdering the Saintess!
But there has to be an explanation for these people.
Th-They must be in disguise! Someone is trying to frame the Paleroches!
Let me see... There must be some gap in their disguise...
Yes! Look at this bolt!
Hmph! These people thought they could fool us wearing Paleroche clothes, but they got a very important thing wrong.
Look, the bolts they're carrying are two-fletched, but the ones used by Paleroche warriors are three-fletched, which are more suitable for battle than hunting.
Dad told me that our family was the best, most loyal warriors of the Paleroches before we split from the clan.
Our shooting skills were passed down from our ancestors, who used them in war, mostly against—
...against the Silverashes.
A logical explanation.
All ancient history now.
The wars between the three clans, Paleroche, Browntail, and Silverash, have long since come to an end, and your family became hunters under Kjeragandr's kind guidance.
Yes... by Kjeragandr...
It's not hard to guess who is impersonating Clan Paleroche.
The Victorians...
Degenbrecher pulls a bolt from one of the Victorian's necks and examines it.
Did you make this yourself?
Yes, my dad taught me when I was little. The craftsmanship has been passed down my family from one generation to the next.
From one generation to the next?
The cut of the fletching, the shape of the point, even the etching on the shaft, these are all unique to Ursus.
How's that possible? Dad said it's a mark of Kjeragandr's faithful.
I've seen arrows like these in the arenas of Kazimierz many times, used by those who claimed to master the most authentic Ursine martial arts.
A gimmick, no doubt, but the GCC also sponsors legitimate historians. I've read their papers and seen the artifacts they dug up.
But... but we're all indigenous to Kjerag. We used to be part of Clan Paleroche!
What does Ursus have to do with Kjerag?
...Enya?
'...Some brought flags, some brought drums, and some brought swords.'
'The three outsiders fought constantly, and their blood turned the rivers red, and She grieved.'
'She went to the place of battle and said—'
'Sheathe your weapons and let your hearts be joined, and I will bestow upon you the blessings of prosperity, and protect you from ill.'
'But the three were foolish, and continued to fight even after they heard Her words.'
'So She cracked the heavens and split the clouds, and She brought forth a miracle, and the fighting was stopped.'
'One of them wept: But how could we forget the hatred, the blood that was shed?'
'And She wept as well, and Her tears froze the enemy, becoming three eternally frozen peaks.'
...What was that?
A passage from the Kjeragandr that is often overlooked... now, I think I understand.
Joanna, I believe that your family... indeed, all of Clan Paleroche, originally came from Ursus.
You think so too?
Yes. But it's not just you. The Silverashes and Browntails came from elsewhere as well.
As the scriptures say, our three clans were outsiders who fought incessantly over resources, until Kjeragandr intervened.

The 'three frozen peaks' seem to refer literally to the three mountains surrounding Kjerag, but it in fact refers to the domains of the three clans, which She had demarcated.
'She will come again, if the swords are not laid down.'
Several years ago, She manifested through me, averting what would have been the first civil war between the three clans in centuries.
The hearts of the three clans must be joined in order to receive Her protection...
The first virtue that Kjeragandr taught us was... forgiveness...
We got them, master!
The tall guards bring over several prisoners, bound with ropes.
These guys were hiding in some abandoned houses, clearly up to no good. They had binoculars and communications equipment on them. Victorians, by the looks of it.
Ha. Just as I thought.
Ratatos is plotting with the Victorians to kill me. We'll take these men to her—
No, we'll do it in front of the Vine-Bead Court with all three clans present, and we'll see what she has to say for herself!
Who's there—
We caught the remaining Victorian spies, Sir Arctosz. We are glad to see that you are unharmed.
What? What do you mean?
Matriarch Ratatos's orders were to protect you, Sir Arctosz.
We had been following you, and stopped these men when they attempted to make a move against you.
The warriors put their bound prisoners down.
What in the name of Kjeragandr...?
...Where is Ratatos?
We do not know.
She said she would try to draw out the spies that Victoria has deployed around here. When they reveal themselves, we can—
The old Browntail manor explodes, its walls torn apart like cardboard.
Glass and wood fragments fly in all directions, as flames consume the traces of a once sumptuous history. The boiling smoke distorts the night air.
The Paleroche warriors stand transfixed, eyes wide. Behind the brown car, some warriors caught by the blast now stagger as they try to help each other up.
The echoes of the explosion gradually disperse, leaving the crackle of wood and distant sirens ever clearer in the night.
A charred jacket drifts down in front of Arctosz.
This is...
...Ratatos?
What the hell have you done?!
A layer of snow has formed on the withered branch. The Rift Cloudbeast climbs to its tip, squinting in an impression of scouting.
The Saintess sighs, turning around to look behind her. The huntress following her pauses, her face slightly red from the wind.
Joanna... you don't need to follow me.
I'm very grateful for all that you've done.
As I told you, though, the Pilgrimage must be completed alone. Strictly speaking, I shouldn't even be talking to you.
I know.
I'm not following you. I'm not your guard or your servant. I just happen to be traveling in the same direction.
Don't worry, I won't talk to you. If anyone sees us, I'll pretend I don't know you.
I won't let danger strike you again. I'll just follow behind.
Do you know where I'm going?
Wherever you're going, I'm going. That's all I need to know.
But... I'm looking for Kjeragandr.
What?
Looking for Her... did She disappear?
I don't know. But disaster will strike Kjerag again if She doesn't return.
People will lose their lives, and even more will lose their faith...
I must return to the Vine-Bead Court and consult the tomes. The others must not know, for now.
Then I'll follow you. Don't worry, if anyone's on your tail, I'll chase them away for you—
Purr—
I think the Rift Cloudbeast just saw something.
In the direction that the Rift Cloudbeast is looking, the trail to the Vine-Bead Court is filled with people dressed in solemn colors.
Looks like... someone very important just passed away.
Who could it be?
Enya shakes her head. She cannot help but feel a shiver down her spine, thick though her clothes are.
Are you alright, Enya?
We'll go slowly, around the crowd.
Dark clouds hang low, almost touching the temple's eaves. It is a cold day even by Kjerag standards, and the north wind seems to penetrate the eyes and spines of the mourners.
Family members and clerics gather in the courtyard with heads bowed. The people of the Browntail domain have gathered outside, to honor and grieve their matriarch, who protected Kjerag with her life.
Snow falls upon the black umbrellas. One of the Browntail nobles gives the one in his hand a little shake, and sighs as the snow pours down.
So, the matriarch's body...
They never found it. The fire was too big. They turned over every inch of whatever was left, but found nothing.
I heard they had to put her usual attire in the coffin, along with some ashes gathered from the scene. Nothing more.
How sad... to think that one as bright as the matriarch would—
M-Madam Sciurus.
...I apologize. I spoke out of turn.
Sciurus does not answer. She walks to the center of the courtyard, waiting for her sister, who had been by her side for long as she could remember.
Chimes tremble to the melody of the flutes. The clerics put their hands together and chant Kjeragandr's hymn, and the people place their hands upon their hearts in prayer to the song—
Kjeragandr have mercy, take her with you.
Six members of Clan Browntail's main branch carry a heavy nanmu wood coffin up the muddy road, Yucatan at the head of the procession.
They carefully lay the coffin down on a table, in front of the new Browntail matriarch.
Yucatan walks to his wife, the grief on his face contrasting with Sciurus's calm expression.
Yucatan...
Rus, I...
Thank you.
The hymn draws to a close. All is quiet and all eyes fall on Sciurus.
For the first time, she understands the mask that Ratatos was forced to wear, the weight of responsibility, which crushes those unworthy of it.
Thank you for being here, to honor the memory of Ratatos Browntail.
She was my sister, staunch guardian of Clan Browntail, and faithful child of Kjeragandr...
Her wisdom gave her courage. She told me that she would protect me and everything here... and she always believed in a better tomorrow for Kjerag, even in the most troubling times.
All her life, she carried out the task that our grandfather entrusted to her, giving everything to Clan Browntail, even in her final moments.
I have lost someone very dear to me. It is only today that I realize the weight of the burden that she carried, but I will never have the chance to tell her.
I know you will remember her face, her voice, her sacrifice, and her deep love... for Kjerag, for Clan Browntail, and for her family.
And you will remember, as I do, who took her from us, leaving behind wounds that can never fully heal.
'A life so short and bright as dawn, shall walk with Her in eternity'...
By Kjeragandr.
'By Kjeragandr,' answer the crowd, following Sciurus' lead as they kneel and pay respect to the departed.
Even in times like these, when all is swept up in calamity, Kjeragandr will continue to bring peace and tranquility to the people of Kjerag...
Two figures emerge from the crowd as those in attendance stand back up. It is Arctosz and Matterhorn, approaching the coffin with white flowers in hand.
My condolences, Sciurus...
Ratatos died because of me. I'll never forget it.
Thank you, Arctosz.
This is a painful lesson to both our clans, to recognize who our true enemies are.
Matriarch Sciurus...

Sir Enciodes expresses his regret for not being able to attend the funeral, and sends his apologies and condolences. He will pay a visit to Browntail Manor personally, when the time is right. Please take care, Matriarch Sciurus.
He's so busy he can't even come to the funeral?
Thank you. I understand. Clan Browntail recognizes the work that Enciodes has done for Kjerag.
Let us continue the ceremony. I believe we have matters to discuss.
The crowd departs. The empty coffin is buried on Browntail land, next to the grandfather who loved her so dearly.
With lips pressed together, Sciurus looks to the two men before her. Both look slightly angry.
I'm simply reiterating the decision of the last Tri-Clan Council, Sir Arctosz: All external affairs shall be handled by Sir Enciodes and Clan Silverash, including matters associated with Victoria.
Is this why you had the nerve to demand the Victorian spies?
The murder of the Browntail Matriarch is no small affair. I won't allow the Silverashes to interrogate them on your own.
Master Matterhorn, it was the Matriarch of Clan Browntail who was assassinated. I believe Clan Browntail should have full authority on this matter.
Is Enciodes not pleased with this arrangement?
Of course not. However, it is a matter of principle to defer to the tradition of the Tri-Clan Council. There is formal and rational justification for allowing Clan Silverash to handle the spies.
We've deferred to Silverash enough already, Matterhorn.
You people have given us nothing but excuses when it comes to negotiating with the Victorians, and now these people have conducted terror attacks in broad daylight.
How about we invite Enciodes to explain his attitude towards the Victorians and what he's planning to Sciurus's face, and then we can talk about the spies.
Kindly refrain from baseless speculation, Sir Arctosz. I have already shown considerable restraint.
You and Matriarch Ratatos were not the only ones to be attacked. Lady Ensia also narrowly escaped with her life. I would like nothing more than to get my hands on the assassins personally.
Well, if that's true, then this will be easy enough.
We'll put the Victorians on trial in front of all Kjerag, and then we let Clan Browntail have their way with them.
Let all Terra know that Kjerag shows no mercy to our enemies.
Too reckless!
We already interrogated them. They were awfully tight-lipped. I doubt a public trial would achieve anything.
Instead, I'm worried they may see that as an opportunity to get a rise out of the people. It's not a risk we can take, with all the unrest in Kjerag right now.
If they have the guts to do so, just cut their tongues out.
We should have dragged them out and spilled their blood for Ratatos at the funeral!
Arctosz... no one wants vengeance for my sister more than I do. But we can't get carried away while there is still the hope of actually coaxing some information from them.
Matriarch Sciurus is right. This is a matter for—
Please excuse the interruption. A cleric from the Vine-Bead Court has something to discuss.
The last envoy from Vine-Bead Court almost got Arctosz and Ensia killed.
How do I know you are who you say you are?
There is no need to be concerned. Sir Arctosz knows me.
He's right, this is Father Frey from the Vine-Bead Court.
Alright then. What is it you want to say, Father?
I am here for the Victorian spies.
The position of the Vine-Bead Court is that this is an extremely delicate issue. There will be considerable criticism and unrest, no matter which of the three clans takes charge.
Therefore, the spies should be placed in the custody of the Court. We shall arrange interrogations, of which you will be informed and in which you'll have the opportunity to participate.
I object. Why delay things when we're going to kill them anyway?
The Vine-Bead Court can't do this. The Tri-Clan Council has already decided that Clan Silverash will handle Victorian affairs.
Need I remind you that the Tri-Clan Council also agreed to return temporal power to the Court?
The Saintess is on her Pilgrimage right now. The Vine-Bead Court cannot decide on her behalf!
It is interesting that you would mention the Saintess now, when Sir Enciodes has always been at odds with us.
All Kjerag knows that the Saintess and the Vine-Bead Court have always been of one mind. The scriptures speak testament to this.
The Vine-Bead Court has often acted on behalf of the Saintess in her absence.
Tell Sir Enciodes he should not presume to take all the power for himself.
Father—
Calm down, Matterhorn.
Clan Browntail accedes to the request. You can send your people tomorrow, Father, and we will hand the spies over to the Vine-Bead Court.
Sciurus?
I know your concerns, but it's more important than ever to put aside the conflicts between the three clans... it would be great to have the Vine-Bead Court mediate.
Thank you for your wisdom, Matriarch. The Vine-Bead Court prays for Clan Browntail.
By Kjeragandr.
Sciurus bows to the Vine-Bead Court envoy.
It is past noon, and the funeral procession begins to disperse.
A man sitting in a car parked a little bit away witnesses the scene, then draws the curtain over the window.
Looks like someone is getting impatient.
There has been a lot of movement in the Vine-Bead Court ever since the Saintess's departure.
I was surprised that Ratatos was willing to go this far.
Using herself as bait to draw out the Victorian spies.
An impressive performance that brought the actions of Victoria into the daylight.
And if the Victorians are trying to instigate a war between her and the other clans, this is also the most effective way to cleanse any suspicion from Clan Browntail.
But it's not enough... we must make good use of the valuable time that Ratatos bought us.
Why didn't you show up at her funeral?
I wanted to avoid direct conflict with the other clan leaders, especially in public.
And... How's Enya?
Her injuries were minor. All the Victorian assassins are dead.
Enya says she has a plan to carry out in secret. She told me to come back, once we cleaned up.
All the people know is that the Saintess is currently on her Pilgrimage. All the Victorians know is that their assassination attempt failed. You've wiped out all the traces from the mountains.
And it's certainly true that the presence of the legendary Black Knight would not be conducive to secrecy.
That's what I thought as well.
Not a bad plan. I also need people I can trust by my side for what is to come.
Our real troubles have only just begun.
Enciodes takes out an envelope and hands it to Degenbrecher. Inside is a thick stack of agreements, and a letter written in Victorian.
Read it.
A letter? From whom?
You could call it a letter...
Or a declaration of war.

lines.st: To the Chairman of Karlan Trade, the son of my kinswoman Elizabeth—
Dear Enciodes Silverash,
I hope this letter finds you well. It has been more than a decade since we met at that banquet.

Perhaps you recalled our conversations, as I did, when you heard about recent events in Victoria. You foretold the conflicts that I was personally involved in, and you have earned the right to sit at the table with Victoria as equals.
But do not forget that every step you have taken is on a path that Victoria wants to see taken, one which she has paved for you.

Victoria's position remains the same after the Catastrophe. We support Kjerag's development and respect Kjerag's faith. And I believe that Clan Silverash and Kjerag have never needed more help than now.
I will offer that help.

Based on the current partnership with Karlan Trade, I will accept the following proposal: A special office jointly created, funded and managed by both sides, establishing a foundation with its location in the city center.
Victoria will double down on existing investment in the form of equipment and interest-free loans, and share more industrial technology to support the future development of Kjerag.
All Kjerag needs to do is to open its doors to Victorian mining efforts, including but not limited to Originium, repaying the loans in kind.

Karlan Trade will not need to provide any cash. After all, there are many places where it can be put to good use, considering present circumstances in Kjerag. I believe this to be a fair sign of good faith.
The associated contracts have already been drafted and enclosed, ready for your review and signature.
I will be happy to meet with you, should you wish to discuss the details.
I look forward to the links that the Caster bloodline can build between us.
And I am curious as to whether Kjerag has become what you wanted it to be.

lines.x: 33 hours before the Kjerag Catastrophe

Instruments, check. Terminal battery, check. Pen ink and paper, check... coffee, check!
It's all-nighter time—
Whoa!
A rustling sound coming from a corner gives Elena a fright. Only then does she realize that she is not the only person in the observation room.
A human figure crawls out of the pile of waste paper by the instruments.
Oh... it's you, Elena.
(Yawns) I thought some great scientist was pulling an all-nighter in an effort to win the Max Prize...
C-Carolin?
You remember me?
Of course I remember the rising star of the Energy Section, the youngest researcher ever to join our ranks.
I-I'm flattered...
I thought you had already left. The schedule said I was supposed to be the one on observation duty today—
Heh, like the schedule's ever meant anything—I wouldn't have been sleeping here for two straight weeks if it did.

No wonder I've hardly seen you ever since I came to the station...
Must be tough.
How does it feel to be back?
You mean...?
Coming back to Rhine Lab.
You were the first person I thought of when they started recruiting for the observation station project, but I thought you would turn it down.
Honestly, it wasn't an easy decision.
At the end of the day, though, the company gave me a valuable opportunity. Besides, the achievements of Rhine Lab itself have never been in question.
I still think my time at Rhine Lab was the closest I've ever been to achieving my dreams.
And I have to admit... even Ferdinand was a great researcher, if nothing else.
Indeed. He has a mind for physics, so let him do what he does best.
You know, he's stopped messing with other researchers since he got out of prison, and has been spending all his time on research.
I think there's going to be a new Director of Energy before long... maybe she's standing in front of me now.
No no no no no no, you've got to be kidding me!
I just wanted to get a closer look...
Elena looks up and sees the sky obscured by snow through the observation's clear dome.
She hates this feeling.
Just like she hates her family's backwards traditions and beliefs, all the things in her way that have nothing to do with science itself.
She believes that science should be pure, ever-advancing, solving questions about Terra one at a time...
This observation station is an invaluable opportunity.
I only want to focus on my research, maybe push a little further than the boundaries of existing knowledge, if I can.
Boundaries, huh... this is the closest place there is.
Say, have you heard the legend of Kjerag?
Legend? What legend?
This land has not suffered a Catastrophe for a thousand years, thanks to the protection of their god.
Well, every country once attributed unusual phenomena to the 'gods'.
Kjerag just held onto this tradition longer because of its isolation.
Yet it is indeed true that Kjerag has not suffered a Catastrophe for a thousand years.
It's certainly unusual... but there must be a reason.
Maybe the high elevation makes it difficult for Originium particles to converge, or—
A Feranmut?
Huh?
Come on, I'm being serious!
I know there are legends about the Feranmuts, ancient beings the size of mountains that turn into mountains and rivers after death.
First off, there are no confirmed accounts proving the existence of Feranmuts. Besides, if these beings did exist, where did they find the energy to sustain their biological activities?
Why bring this up, anyway?
Just something that occurred to me while we were chatting.
Well, that's enough gabbing. You can rest, I'll take over observations for the night.
Really? But you've done a bunch of night shifts in a row—
It's not like my circadian rhythm can get any more messed up. And chatting with you woke me up.
If I recall correctly, there are still some bugs to iron out with the power supply to the high-energy particle beam?
Wow, y-you know about that?
Get some rest and put that brain of yours to better use.
Well, I guess I'll take you up on the offer! Thank you so much!
I'll buy you some fins and chips from that Victorian restaurant that just opened at the foot of the mountain when you have time!
Please don't, no Victorian wants to have to enjoy authentic Victorian cuisine in a foreign land.
Hehe... Kjerag cheese fondue, then?
That sounds great.
It is another deep and quiet night in Kjerag. The ancient land slumbers, and lamps give way to stars.
The sleeping snowy mountains may seem wondrous to the uninitiated, though most who live on this land have gotten used to this sight.
Few look up at the sky on a night like this.
What's that...?!
Both her eyes and the instruments capture the strange light that cut across the night sky.
Like a sign from the heavens.
It appears that Carolin was an accomplished, intelligent young researcher with a bright future.
This project is very important to both Rhine Lab and Columbia. The recruiting process was extremely selective.
We have yet to find any irregularities in her records and communication logs.
She was originally from Victoria?
I am unable to provide any private information. That would be a violation of Rhine Lab's privacy policy, and basic human rights.
We can only provide information about her time at Rhine Lab, unless your government officially presses charges of espionage.
Of course. I was just asking.
Her application material was very comprehensive, with every shift and fieldwork experience documented meticulously...
Here, it seems she even requested a field survey task for herself.
Collecting information about Kjerag's geography concurrently with her scientific research?
She has been a diligent researcher, highly rated at Rhine Lab, and a model student when she was at school.
Such scientists are not usually involved in the kind of trouble that we currently face.
Yet, at this point, we have no information or clue as to the whereabouts of Carolin.
Believe it or not, we are even more eager to find Carolin than you are.
There are many things at stake. Rhine Lab's reputation, the security of the research data, potential breach of our management system, the mutual trust and partnership between us, and...
And?
...And Kjerag's development.
Are you speaking as a member of the Scientific Ethics Consensus Committee?
Please don't misunderstand. Though it might sound a little condescending, I have the highest respect for what Karlan Trade is doing.
After all, the courage to reach beyond one's comfort zone is the most important quality in a scientist.
Not just in scientists.
There is a saying in Kjerag, 'Black burdenbeasts are bad burdenbeasts.'
They jump their fences and run into the snowfields. Herders who go into the fields following their tracks never return.
They die?
That's what 'go into the snowfields and never return' usually means in Kjerag.
One by one, pages are reviewed and placed on the table—Carolin's notes and actions turn into a tiny pile of papers.
But where did the black burdenbeasts go? It's the same question, in the end.
Perhaps they led the herders into the mountains, getting lost in the forests and freezing to death at night.
Another page is read and placed on the table.
Or perhaps...
Gnosis puts the last page down.
White upon white, like snowflakes falling on frozen cliffs.
They simply found a new source of water, a new haven.
Can you at least tell me where exactly she carried out her fieldwork?
I can't. The observation data are badly corrupted. I can't recover the original data from the port...
Stacks of documents and mugs with dried coffee stains sit quietly on the table.
An error tone signifies failure of the data restoration, causing their hearts to sink once more.
There are ID records of all entries and exits, no message left behind in the dorms or labs...
The main security camera malfunctioned, while the two to the sides of the lab didn't capture anything...
It seems like too much of a coincidence. Could Carolin really have...?
No. Everyone else might assume she's a spy, but I can't let bias cloud my judgment. I already requested access permissions from Director Muelsyse to get to the bottom of things...
Knock, knock. Who's been up all night?
D-Director Muelsyse?
Surprised to see me?
I hear you haven't been back to the dorms for two straight nights... are you still searching for the data that disappeared?
Yes...
There is little doubt that the observation logs were altered, although we're not sure who did it, or why.
There is reason to believe that the overwritten data hold a crucial clue...

Hm... but...
Never mind. I brought you a li'l snacky. Can't work on an empty stomach, after all.
Thank you. What is this?
You've never had it? Kjerag cheese pastries with sugar frosting and berry jam. It was all the rage at the station a while back.
I heard people talking about it and had planned on trying them out the next time I went down the mountain for fondue, but I've been too busy investigating special energy sources unique to Kjerag.
Elena picks up a pastry. It's dried up on the outside, but the cheese and jam inside remain moist after being heated.
The sound of running water ceases, and Muelsyse sits down by her side with two clean mugs in hand.
Mind if I sip a cuppa joe with you?
Not at all.
Fresh coffee fills the mugs, drenching the air in its aroma.
The sound of machines operating is clearly audible at this late hour. A massive window on the wall displays the stars in Kjerag's night sky, deep and boundless as always.
The atmosphere in the room relaxes. Elena lifts her mug after a moment of hesitation.
Midnight coffee in the lab. Brings back memories.
You used to stay up late too?
Who doesn't, in our line of work?
Even when I was a student, it felt like I had more coffee than blood in veins for finals—
Haha... Looks like everyone's been there before.
Working at night's more efficient, after all.
Yes. Night is quiet. Makes it easier to focus.
I used to write one paper while dictating the summary of another using an audio recorder, when I was really busy.
That way, I get to make progress on both at the same time. Crazy, when I think back to it...
Oh yeah! Audio recorder!
Huh? Audio? Where are you going?
Surveillance audio recordings!
Surveillance camera, credentials... the time is... 3:19 am...
There.
Didn't you say the cameras didn't capture what was happening at the lab table?
Yes... but it might have captured sound from the equipment.
Listen to this... a lot of instruments operating. This ticking sound should be the audio sensor.
The frequency that it plays back is directly connected to graphing. We can use this frequency to recreate—excuse me.
Need a pen and paper? Here you go.
Oh, thanks.
Pen tip rubs against paper. The time they spend around the instruments have made researchers intimately familiar with data.
She enters the numbers that she copied down into the computer, then...
There...
This is the hidden data.
Lemme take a look-see...
Looks like a small earthquake occurred on Mount Karlan the morning of the day before the Catastrophe.
No... not an earthquake. It was an impact of some kind.
As if something fell from the sky into the mountains of Kjerag.
And then... came Kjerag's first Catastrophe.
Outside the wide windows of the observation station, the mountains of Kjerag seem to reflect a translucent blue under the moon.
Did Carolin go looking for this extraterrestrial object?
But why would she do it all on her own?
She's never abandoned her post like this. She must have found something...
We might be able to find her, if we figure out where that thing fell!
Which we can calculate from the wave frequency of the sound of impact!
Director Muelsyse, we...
The Vine-Bead Court isn't about to investigate on our behalf.
Strictly speaking, we shouldn't get any more involved in any of this stuff. The most we can do is to report this to Component Control.
But Carolin might be in danger...
You know that's not your job.
You're not exactly close to Carolin. Bluntly, whatever happens to her is none of your business.
Muelsyse finishes her cold coffee in silence.
Frowning at the bitter taste, she takes a piece of pastry and puts it in her mouth, speaking as she chews.
...But.
Sometimes, I kinda think the most important quality in a scientist is courage... maybe even recklessness.
You're a sharp kiddo. Got a knack for finding rhythm in music.
Makes for a good dancer. I wouldn't wanna tie you up, gotta let you move around free.
