There's a light ahead; it must be the path leading to the main ore vein. Come—let's hold hands, just like before.
We are just a few steps shy of our answer. We mustn't run into danger here.
Soon... We will find all our answers.
The map is illuminated by the flickering light of the oil lamp. They hardly converse, moving forward in silence, their steps filled with both fear and anticipation.
But eventually, the search comes to an end.
...Are you sure this is the right place? Might we have made a mistake?
No... I'm sure of it.
This is the mine I previously calculated and located... There's no way this could be the wrong place.
But...
What are you two talking about? This is the place we were looking for, yeah?
According to my plan, we should be able to find an Originium vein here... My deduction can be proven correct, if and only if it exists...
But, this place...
...is an Oriron mine.
What...?
I've investigated and analyzed everything; these are all just ordinary Oriron deposits. There's no chance they're related to Catastrophe...
...Let alone any traces of 'extraterrestrial objects'. Where did we go wrong?
But, why?! We clearly saw a bunch of trucks transporting Originium on our way here! How could this all just be Oriron?
It doesn't take long for Enya to grasp the schemes of the man bonded to her by blood.
Indeed... We came to the wrong place.
The mineral vein here is not the 'Heart of Karlan', but rather, the 'Bones of Kjerag'...
The bones that uphold Kjerag's spine...
...Um, may I say something?
The Heart of Karlan is a pure core that validates faith. Man's eyes, ears, mouth, nose, and hands can deceive, and only the heart cannot. Such is written in the Kjeragandr.
But, I don't think we're being unfaithful or impious.
We all know how much we've done... and surely, She must know too.
Just like how give everything on the hunt, only to come up empty-handed and go hungry through the night.

But, we must have faith. Faith in the land of Kjerag. Faith in Kjeragandr's protection. She promised us that as long as we have our two hands, we shall not starve or freeze to death in Kjerag's lands.
So, Great Saintess, come. Let's try again––
Huh? Try... what?
Use your bell to call Kjeragandr.
That sweet bell—the sound of the Saintess who played to awaken Kjeragandr, the clear sound beloved of Her, the sound that is Her call.

So it says in the Kjeragandr that burdenbeasts wearing this bell will not be snatched away by wild beasts; they shall not fall ill or die young, and shall find their way back home should they ever be lost.
For one's 'heart to be astray' is to be lost too, right? I don't totally get what it means, but I heard it from one of the clerics at the Vine-Bead Court!
Unlike her two dejected companions, the girl shows no signs of discouragement and is eager to try again.
That's right. We should keep trying. Perhaps Kjeragandr will respond to us.
The silver bell rings out on high, its engraved patterns shimmering beneath the hazy light of the miner's lamps.
She had tried to call out like this before. At the Vine-Bead Court, in her own bedroom, among her books, while resting among the snow-capped mountains.
Ring—ring—
The clear, cold sound of the silver bell echoes through the empty cave.
...Woah, that's...!
This sound, this frequency... Why does it sound so alike?!
Did you discover something?
That frequency! The echoes of your bell throughout the cave sound just like that one audio recording from the observatory—almost identical, even!
Is that just a coincidence?
A coincidence? No...
'In nature, no two snowflakes are exactly alike'...
Say, Saintess... Where exactly did that bell come from?
That would require going back to very ancient legends...
In Kjerag history, there was a long span of time where Kjeragandr fell into deep slumber until She was awoken by a devout Saintess.
It was Kjeragandr's gift to that Saintess.
I've also heard that when subsequent craftsmen made bells for the Saintesses, they sought to imitate the sounds of the mountains...
The sounds of the mountains...? What kind of mountain could make a sound––
A Feranmut?
Impossible... How could that be...
Snap out of it, Elena... You're a scientist. How could you let your faith falter at a time like this...?
Elena, are you saying that on the day before the Catastrophe, something fell onto Mount Karlan, thus creating that sound?
Based on my previous conjectures, yes...
So think about it this way... If we think of the mountain as a huge bell, doesn't that mean there's probably a huge hollow inside it?
Theoretically speaking, that would... be possible... But, do you know where a massive hollow like that could exist?
There is a rumor not recorded in Kjerag's official history, and it also happens to be related to the Saintess who awakened Kjeragandr.
A long, long time ago, there once was an uprising against the Vine-Bead Court in Kjerag.

The people had grown dissatisfied because Kjeragandr had not performed any miracles in a long time, and so they marched upon the Vine-Bead Court. Their wrath was only quelled when the Saintess awakened the sleeping deity.
During the chaos, a number of clerics took their precious scriptures and took refuge within Mount Karlan, therefore preserving some of their valuable knowledge.
Yet regardless, the classic works of the Vine-Bead Court still suffered a break in continuity around that time.
What if... the account of Kjeragandr leaving her people within the scriptures... is actually a description of this event?
Great Saintess... If this sanctuary truly exists, will we find traces of the 'Kjeragandr' there?
Where should we even look?
Behind the Vine-Bead Court... the place leading to Mount Karlan's summit.
Enya tightly grips the silver bell in her hand. She seems to hear the wind and the snow, as well as the footsteps that would occasionally shadow her while she worked at the Vine-Bead Court.
Whose voice was that? Who was calling to her from behind? Who would pat her on the shoulders and bring honeyed cystymilk to her while she was burning the midnight oil to organize her scriptures?
All she can hear and see is the pure white snow that never stops blowing with the wind.
O Kjeragandr on high, please grant us your guidance.
May the path we walk be true.
'A cleric from the Vine-Bead Court, in collusion with Victorian agents, stole important military intelligence and was arrested yesterday.'
'Great Elder Adso confessed to some of these allegations, and agreed to fully cooperate with the investigation. During this time, all activities of the Vine-Bead Court are to be suspended.'
'There is considerable evidence suggesting that Victorian agents are involved in a string of unusual natural and social phenomena.'
'Karlan Trade is also assembling scientific researchers to assess the possibility of an espionage-related Catastrophe.'
'As a reminder to all citizens of Kjerag: If any foreign national approaches you under the guise of offering charity, business ventures, or gathering news, please be extremely vigilant...'
I knew it!
The Great Elder and that Victorian duke are in cahoots! Look what those bastards have brought upon us!
Couldn't it just be one big misunderstanding? If even the Vine-Bead Court has betrayed Kjeragandr... My goodness...
They've been mucking about with the Victorians, and you still give them the benefit of the doubt?
But, hasn't the Karlan Trade you seem to trust so much been dealing with Victoria this whole time...?
The only reason you can even hear all this on the radio is thanks to Karlan Trade. But what about the Vine-Bead Court? What've they ever done for you?
Stop your bickering. Victorian broadcasting has a response. Shut up and listen.
I don't give a damn what they have to say!
...I need to make sense of all of this.
'Sir Wilson, you are highly respected by Her Grace the Duke, as well as an expert on the Kjerag affair. We would like to ask you—how should we interpret the attitude displayed by Enciodes Silverash?
'I see it as nothing more than a way of quelling the public discontent in Kjerag. Enciodes has always been a close partner of Victoria.'
'Don't you find that somewhat difficult to believe?'
'Even based on publicly-available information, it is an indisputable fact that Victoria has been the primary beneficiary of many of the trade contracts strongly spearheaded by Enciodes himself.'

'With the help of Karlan Trade, Kjerag's development has almost entirely come off the back of Victoria's aid, from the import of breeding beasts and fertilizers to railway technology and even Originium processing.'
'In return, Enciodes has committed to handing over more than forty percent of Kjerag's natural resources for Victoria's development.'
'Not along ago, Karlan Trade also handed over a mining site that was being worked by Kazimierz to the Duke of Caster.'
'Then, it seems that Enciodes is trying to maintain an attitude of ambiguity. That being the case, are you not worried that his attempts to soothe the public will anger the Duke?'

'Lest you forget, Enciodes is the Duke's grand-nephew, and the blood of Caster flows in his veins. The delicate understanding between him and the Duke is, without a doubt, difficult for outsiders to grasp.'

'What you may not know is that even while he issued the statement, he also submitted a new mining agreement to the Duke, who has already signed and returned it. Expect a joint announcement on this matter in short time.'
'I have obtained the key points of this agreement and would like to share them with you here. Do take a look at these graphs, good sir.'
''Karlan Trade is willing to entrust 100% of future mining rights to a foundation established jointly with the Duke of Caster'. This truly is...'
'Just the tip of the iceberg. Look at what comes next...'
Nonsense!
The young reporter is startled by the volume of her outburst. She instinctively reaches to pat her bulging backpack before turning around.
Seeing the empty street makes her let out a heavy sigh.
Phew... Looks like they won't be able to catch up to me.
Camera, recorder, film prints... Everything's here. This time, I won't let them take these away!
She then looks at the statue of Kjeragandr in the distance, pulls down the brim of her hat, and walks toward the city's center.
The empty silence leaves one feeling colder than ever. The reporter's heavy bag slows her down, her footsteps upon the snow clear for all to hear.
Less people than before, even here...
...Hmm?
Miss! Sorry to bother you, can I ask you a few quick questions?
About?
Right now, Karlan Trade—
I'd rather not have to hear that name.
Sir Enciodes needs to come out and go over why he signed those agreements already.
'Resources for development', what rubbish. All we got for our resources was a Catastrophe. How's he going to explain that, huh?
By the time the reporter finishes digesting the lady's angry words, she has already long-since left with a swish of her cloak.
...Excuse me, sir!
Ah, you're one of those reporters from Victoria?
Kjerag's not been the kindest place, lately. Even us locals prefer holing up at home. You best take care.
It's times like these where I can't be afraid, otherwise I'm not fit to hold this camera.
...But thanks for the heads-up, anyhow. What I'd like to know is, how do you see Enciodes right now, in your honest opinion?
Hmm... Sorry, Miss, I think he's cheated Kjerag, but Victoria's worth doubting too.
I've seen how big the changes here have been these past few years. If you said Enciodes hasn't had any difficulty making those changes, then I'd say you're wrong.
If only the Great Elder of the Court could come out and say a few words, it'd all be fine—
A stone flies right past the tip of the reporter's nose all of a sudden, smashing the store window behind her.
Eek—!
Get out of Kjerag, Victorian scum!
Enciodes, the Vine-Bead Court, us, we've all got nothing to do with you!
Still got the stones to take pictures?! Get the faen off our land already!
Nothing has left her with complicated feelings more than this moment.
She has seen the ordinary people of Kjerag slowly become more mistrustful, more on-edge.
Yet she has also seen once-trusted members of the camera crew sneak into her room to inspect her drafts and camera, over and over.
As the man picks up yet another stone, she bows goodbye to her interviewee, and runs away from the scene...
It seems her only choice is to escape... but escape to where?
Are you Victorian, miss?
Ah... Hello there.
Do... you hate Victoria?
Nope, I don't.
A dirty little hand reaches out to her, with a piece of stale Victorian bread sitting in the palm.
But my pappa told me to throw these all away. He said we wouldn't have any Victorian charity in our house.
Then...
But Kjeragandr taught us to love our neighbors, and repay kindness with kindness.
So, I wanted to give it back to you.
As she watches the child hurry away, the reporter feels as if the dry bread in her hand is choking her throat. Her eyelids tremble, and she clenches her teeth...
Get her in the car. Don't let anyone see.
That is the last thing she remembers hearing before she loses consciousness.
Sorry for bothering you with yet another visit, Mr. Silverash.
Perhaps you would rather be seeing some other pretty face during all this hubbub...
There is no need to be so humble.
Kjerag is indebted to you for your care, and I had hoped for the chance to express my gratitude.
Hardly, hardly... It seems my long-awaited return has left me awash with churning emotion once more.
I would like nothing more than to be the bearer of good news every time I come over, before throwing my old bones into the mountains for some peace and quiet.
But life is a fickle thing, and it demands we improvise, does it not?
I do believe you are here to give me some bad news, Lord Viscount, but not in the name of Caster.
Hmph, I'd rather not get mixed up with that old lady's affairs again.
Though based on what went on the last time I was here... It's her who doesn't want to get mixed up with me.
With all that said, My Lord...
Ah, yes, on to business, I know you're a busy man.
I am here to bid you farewell, Mr. Silverash.
If the recent shift in public opinion against Victoria has troubled you, then I can—
No, no, no, nothing of the sort...
...I had hoped against hope that old Caster was also reaching the age where she spoke from the heart.
That tends to bring a bit of sweetness to the tongue, no? But as you can see, things have come to this.
Though I may seem a dotard these days, I still find myself compelled to ask you one thing...
Is there truly no other way?
You know her plans involve more than just a few mines, My Lord.
From the moment she laid hands on Ensia, this negotiation has become wholly unacceptable to me.
So how do you plan to handle her? Are you really prepared to cross swords with her?
You know better than I that if we do not keep the idea of 'crossing swords' on the table, then Kjerag may as well already be in her pocket.

Of course I do...
*sigh* What to say, what to say...
Knowing how the old lady is, she will never let your little tiff go now that it's reached this stage.
I truly am worried for you, Sir Enciodes. Whatever shall you do next?
What is there to do but to seize the opportunity?
Since you have mentioned her, you are also an old acquaintance of Her Grace, are you not?

Me?
Hardly know her.
A few years back, we'd chat about family matters, and she'd always grumble to me about how none of the young Casters were worth a damn.
The less useful they turn out to be, the more hasty she gets in doing things. Who would have guessed that she was known as a complete enigma in her youth...
I had a brief encounter with Her Grace a decade or so ago. Her grandeur strikes me even now.
Hah, no need to butter her up to me.
I am fully aware that her high-and-mighty noble posturing is something she can never change.
No matter how many schemes are brewing behind the scenes, one must always keep one's face haughty and dignified.
Well, that's that. I'm sure your people have more urgent things to trouble you with. I should be heading off right about now.
This talk of ours has been productive, My Lord.
I hope I will have a more suitable opportunity in the future to invite you to Kjerag.
Haha, couldn't ask for better!
Ah, yes...
(Quietly) When you next see Her Grace, by no means tell her I've been calling her 'old' behind her back.
...I would never, Lord Viscount.
Atta boy.
I too, am really looking forward to the chance to visit Kjerag again.
Yes, nobody saw us.
It was no trouble, Matriarch. Your request is my—
I'll bring her over right away.
Hey, wakey-wakey!
...I...This is... I was at—
Half a day's sleep not enough for you? Get up, our Matriarch has things she wants to ask you.
What...?
Who... brought me here...?
Hmph. How long will it take you to learn a gentle touch?
I-I'm sorry.
Matriarch.
Hey, you awake yet?
No thrashing about, unless you feel like the ropes around your wrists aren't chafing enough.
Ah, Ratatos!
...Matriarch Ratatos. I thought you were—
Not a single burn on me. Real surprising, isn't it?
When you get the chance, tell your colleagues that when dealing with the Browntails, best check the room for tricks.
My... colleagues?
Be honest now, my little spy. What were you hoping to capture on that camera of yours, all those days you were sneaking about?
Wait, what... No, you misunderstand me, Madam! How could I be a spy?!
*sigh*...
That's what all the other spies we got said too.
The guards rifle through the reporter's back and throw its contents onto the floor: the photos she has taken and developed the past few days.
We wouldn't care much about you if you take pictures of local traditions or even patrolling guards.
But you didn't hesitate before taking a bunch of pictures of the Vine-Bead Court. You even went to a few mountains, clicking that shutter of yours.
Don't think I don't know anything.
Matriarch Ratatos, whether you believe me or not, I really am nothing more than a regular reporter.
If I was a spy, why would I be walking around with all this evidence on me?
With all this chaos going on right now, I could have easily given it all to my colleagues at the TV station.
That's what you need to explain.
Fact of the matter is, you were carrying a great deal of suspicious material, and had a very strange itinerary. How do I know you weren't trying to flee?
Because I'm trying to protect that material!
The Duke of Caster wants control over public opinion. The news office sent people to join the camera crew, and they've been sneaking into my room to edit my pictures.
If I didn't try to get away, most of them would be confiscated, maybe even used to misrepresent things here.
Everything they do is to whitewash Victoria trying to strong-arm its way into Kjerag!

If you think these pictures will be of any use to Kjerag, you can have them all.
I just want you to release them, to let more people know what's going on here...
It doesn't need Victoria's 'support', but real, actual help!
That might be tricky.
Is Enciodes able to take a call right now?
He has more pressing matters to handle, so he sent me as support.

After my lovely funeral, I handed the spies we captured over to him. According to our agreement, he was supposed to send backup.
And in the end, he only sent one woman, even if she is the Black Knight.
Not sure if I should call him generous or stingy here...
So, what's his plan for dealing with Caster?
We're still gathering information on our enemy. I was also sent here to help with your interrogations.
The most critical thing to find out now, is if this reporter has taken any photos of 'things in the mountains'.
Ah, yes, our little friend here...
I can untie your restraints, but first, you need to promise me...
You'll answer all my questions. That sound alright to you?
An empty office.
Gnosis is unused to the sight. He walks to his desk. Under his mug, filled with steaming-hot fresh coffee, is a document with Enciodes's signature.
'Letter of Appointment'.
Would it have killed you to come over and say goodbye...?
...It is signed.
You know how risky this is.
Minerals and land are inseparable. We shall bypass the Vine-Bead Court and sign cooperative mining agreements with foreign nations ourselves.
This will cause a lot of headaches for us in the future.
If we do not pay them their pound of flesh now to close the gap, they shall simply demand much more from us later.
This is the lowest price we are able to bear.
Your calculations are rather optimistic, Enciodes.
And you have always been less optimistic than I am, no matter the topic.
Which means most times, you have been able to walk further than me.
If it wasn't for that optimism of yours, I would not have the confidence to take a single step in the darkness.
Heh.
It has been a long while since I heard such praise from you.
Go, see our plans through, Gnosis.
Everyone from middle management and above is here. We can start the camera at any time.
Begin recording.
Yes, sir. What is today's announcement about?
I have gathered you all here today to announce a major change in personnel.
As of today, Enciodes Silverash will no longer serve as Chairman and CEO of Karlan Trade.
Enciodes admits all responsibility for the recent occupational incidents and has resigned. The Board of Directors has already ratified his request.
At the same time, the Board has decided that I, Gnosis Edelweiss, shall take over all of Enciodes's duties.
Now, allow me to make the first proclamation of Karlan Trade's new era as a representative of the Board, to all our colleagues, and to the people of Kjerag.
Karlan Trade shall be ceasing all collaboration with Victoria.
As for the many past agreements that Enciodes insisted on signing, they shall all be temporarily paused until the Board has decided on further courses of action.

At this extraordinary time, I ask all our colleagues to work hard at their posts, to keep the development of Kjerag at the forefront of their minds, and to sweep away the lingering problems of the past.
...
Kjerag needs us.
Wake up, we're here for our shift.
...Sure took your time.
First time we've been here, had to find the place.
This lot been much trouble?
Most of them aren't talking much. Just mind the lead geezer.
Which one's he?
Isn't the Vine-Bead Court all geezers?
Everyone bursts out in laughter before heading off, leaving just the two guards to take their shift.
...You should ease it up a bit. How could you disrespect the elders like that? You're from Kjerag too, right?
Still sticking up for him? It was this bunch of Schiisdräck who got us into this mess—
Shut it, you got a death wish?
The two guards look towards Elder Adso as he sits within his cell. He is focused on the thick tome in his hands, playing with a corner of the page as he thinks.
All's good.
What're you so afraid of? They've been talking big for centuries now, but it's not like the Court's managed to flip Kjerag upside-down, have they?
They're the vicars of Kjeragandr. Even if you don't respect the elders, you should still respect Her.
What's the Court done that a guy like me needs to respect them?
When my mom was sick, the clerics picked some grass for her to eat. Made me pray to the grass, saying that it would bring Kjeragandr's protection.
In the end, we brought her to one of Karlan Trade's doctors. He told me, if we had kept going with the clerics' 'treatment plan', she wouldn't have lasted another day.
I'd say the real vicar is Sir Enciodes!
...'The scholar known as Jürgen was an ardent follower of Her, and did much good. Out of resentment, evil men accused him of stealing courtly coin, and he was to be put to death a few days hence.'
'Jürgen's limbs were bound tight, and at night, he heard the clattering of soldiers' swords. He knew his death was near, and in his heart, he was anguished.'
This is...
Guess I found which geezer to look out for.
The guard raises his baton as he approaches Adso's cell, striking the bars over and over. Adso does not stop his recitation as he looks at the guard calmly.
Hey, greybeard, shut your mouth while you're in that cell.
Think about how you're going to explain all your sins to Kjeragandr when you meet Her.
'Jürgen looked up but saw no light. In his futility, he knelt upon the straw lining his cell, in supplication to Her.'
'O Righteous Kjeragandr, have I not done good in Your name? Have I not sought truth in Your name?'
'Do good men die in Your kingdom while the unjust gain salvation?'
'Why do the lawmakers only hear absurd falsities? Why do the people I saved stand in unspeaking silence?'
Schiisdräck! Have you no shame, you traitor?!
I'm going to teach you a lesson—
The guard puts his baton away before taking out his key to open the cell door, though it keeps missing the keyhole as his right hand trembles in rage.
...'O Kjeragandr! Though my body be trapped in gaol, my soul still seeks Your mercy.'
'Whilst my eyes can still see, and my ears can still hear, stretch out Your righteous hands to me!'
I swear, today is going to be the last day you can even open a copy of the Kjeragandr!
Wh-What have I done...
I hurt another guard...
You have done well, my child.
Our divine duty is not limited to the three clans, the Saintess, or the Vine-Bead Court.
All children of Kjerag must be ready to carry out Her will at any time.
Just like you did.
I remember... 'Jürgen the Confessor'.
When I was young, I was lost in the snowy mountains. Just as I was about to black out, a cleric saved me.
When we were on his burdenbeast, he took off his coat and wrapped it tight around me, telling me the story of Jürgen, and how Kjeragandr will save all the innocents...
Where are the other clerics imprisoned, my child?
Nearby.
Find an opportunity to free them. Let them escape, then save yourself.
Conflict brews atop the mountains. The Vine-Bead Court shall rebuild everything upon the ruins.
Allow me to tell you the end of the tale of Jürgen the Confessor.
Halt!
Don't force my hand, Great Elder. We are armed, and we will not allow anyone to leave!
...I will walk by you.
If you insist on using bows and crossbows to run counter to Her will, then aim at your own hearts. Try it.
You...
The guards who rushed over look at their comrades with raised eyebrows as they form two lines. Many of them have chosen to stow their weapons, opening up a narrow passage.
Adso lowers his head as he walks through the guards, leaving behind faint footprints in the snow.

lines.st: 'As the words left Jürgen's mouth, the entire gaol began to sway, as wind and snow entered through the cracks.'
'The walls around the imprisoned devotee were as rotten trees, shattering into dust as his body was enveloped in blinding light.'
'The soldiers were thrown to the ground, their weapons shattered, but they themselves were unharmed. They prostrated themselves at Jürgen's feet, weeping as they confessed their sins.'
'Jürgen the Confessor then walked the lands of the three clans, preaching this miracle the rest of his life.'
'And thus, Kjeragandr stretches out Her hand to those who call for Her, to enact Her justice.'

lines.x: She has slowly lost all sensation in her joints as her shoes sink into the soft snow, step after step after step, yet her legs do not stop moving, even as she gulps lungfuls of frigid air.
Hah.... hah....
...Who's... Hah...
There is the vague shape of people in the distance. They draw close to her, but the lights flashing before her eyes make her unable to recognize them.
You...
We have been ordered to receive you, Madam Carolin.
Though there should be others with you here.
They got taken by the locals.
Then, are there any issues with the intelligence?
You can tell Her Grace that the power hiding behind Kjerag has disappeared.
I've observed and recorded the fall of some extraterrestrial object to the surface. The ensuing Catastrophe is further proof of my conclusion.
Whether it's the energy field theory or the Feranmut hypothesis, it explains the changes that happened to Kjerag.
Take this folder, all my research into several hypotheses about Kjerag is in it. You can—
What the—?!
I am terribly sorry, Madam.
Did you really believe you could hide your personal communications with the Maylander Foundation from us?
Her Grace has asked us to bring back only the data.
I believe she is very interested in the fact that Kjerag has lost its protection.
You—
Extraterrestrial objects, Feranmut legends...
There are still so many marvelous things we don't know the truth about across this great land.

But all you care about is deception, robbery and murder. Everything else is worthless to you.
If I didn't need you to escape from Kjerag...
I would never work with people like you!
I shall pass on your complaint to Her Grace, verbatim.
Goodbye, Madam. Thank you for your patriotic 'service'.
She sighs deeply, unsure of whom she is actually feeling sorry for.
A smoke grenade?
Enemy attack! Look for cover!
The night is enveloped in smoke and gunfire, leaving the snowfield in utter chaos.
At least, until a black off-roader courses out of the smoke. In its wake are the Victorian agents, futilely shooting at the vehicle as it drives into the distance.
Sorry to put you through that, Carolin.
Had to take our time so the Victorians wouldn't spot us.
Got all your stuff?
She lowers her head, pulling out a marked file on the extraterrestrial object from the various documents before handing it to the agent sitting in the front seat.
...So, when can I start my research?
No need to be so hasty, Miss. My boss wants to have a chat with you first. He's really interested in hearing what you're studying.
I think you'll be working with us for a long, long time.
Carolin nods her head, before she suddenly realizes something.
She never had the chance to actually hand over Victoria's 'Kjerag report'.

She rolls down the car window, heedless of the cold wind blowing in. She takes out the papers she so painstakingly wrote in Kjerag, one after the other, and throws them out the window with nary a moment's hesitation.
The paper is all the more white under the black night, like fresh snow falling upon the wilds.
They spin through the air, buoyed by cold wind and political maelstrom both.
Here's your things back, Victoria.
So according to you, that Duke Caster has had her spies embedded in Kjerag for a long, long time. And it's all to prepare for a future annexation?
But how is she going to do that?
To be more precise... this is my educated guess based on what I've learned in Kjerag.
So out of all the Victorians sneaking around Kjerag, you happen to be the only actual reporter who's after truth and righteousness and suchlike?
Well, you don't have to be so rude about it!
My name's Alberta. If you're familiar with the Victorian newsprint business, you would've heard that name in conjunction with many articles about the truth that were deemed 'unprintable'.
I take pride in my work, no matter what they say.
Hmph...
I'll believe you for now, little Miss Reporter, but this doesn't mean you're completely free of suspicion.
I've had my guards rip out the spies your people sent after the explosion.
If you're telling the truth, then take us to your crew's station. I've got a lot of things I'd like to ask the agents you mentioned.
You wouldn't refuse me, would you?
But—
Move!
Degenbrecher grabs Ratatos and the reporter, pulling them into the back room as a stun grenade from who-knows-where goes off in the interrogation room.
In the distance, a Browntail guard who was unable to get out of the way in time screams. A throwing knife has pierced his heart.
Dammit!
Degenbrecher pokes her body out the doorway, hiding back behind the door as another knife flies towards her. She watches it tremble after it embeds itself in the door frame.
These knives are the ones used by the Trilby Ashers.
You two stay hidden here. I'll go confirm the scale of the raid and their strategy.

As she finishes speaking, she suddenly draws her twin weapons, knocking two knives thrown from the shadows out of the air before springing forward to attack. The dust she kicks up forces the reporter to squint.
You—
What the hell's going on? How do the Trilby Ashers know about this place?
Did you leak it to them? But I've been watching you the whole time, that can't be...
I didn't! I even checked over all my things, and made sure nobody was following me.
Look out!
The room shakes violently. The reporter pulls at Ratatos's sleeve, moving her to the other side of the room as fragments of the damaged ceiling smash into the floor where they just were.
Then how do you explain all this?
...Wait! There's still one place I haven't checked.
She picks up the camera hanging around her neck, removes the lens, presses down on the shutter, and lifts up the reflector. A faint red light glows within.
She pulls out the suspicious component. A miniature tracking device.
So that's where it was hiding...
Another explosion, and thick smoke slowly seeps into the room the two are hiding in. Degenbrecher emerges from the smoke, her breathing somewhat ragged.
Three Trilby Ashers with this first group. I fended them off for now, but the reinforcements they called for are already on their way here.
I will keep you safe, Ratatos, but we need to leave.
Let's go to the Vine-Bead Court. There's a mountain trail nearby that leads to one of the secret passages in.
They won't dare act rashly against the Court. It'll buy us some time.
Wait, I need to come with you.

Since the Trilby Ashers will keep chasing us, I'll make a record of all their assassination attempts in Kjerag and compile them together with everything they've done so far. I'll pass them to a local friend to get the word out.
This will expose all of Victoria's dirty deeds to the public.
I can't let myself be taken in by them!
Enough babbling, I still need to find Enciodes for a joint questioning.
You'd be coming with us even if you didn't want to.
...Yes, Ratatos is alive. The three of them have entered the Vine-Bead Court's northern gate.
The escaped reporter is with her, and the renowned Black Knight is guarding them.
What orders from Her Grace?
...Wait.
...Did you just say that Her Grace will personally be visiting Kjerag?

lines.st: To Karlan Trade Headquarters
and to Enciodes Silverash, son of the former Chairman and my kinswoman Elizabeth
alongside all the people of Kjerag:
This is an open letter, within which is written all the wrongdoings of Enciodes and Karlan Trade both.
With this letter I shall formally protest, protest all the grievances that Victoria and Kjerag share in suffering from, that were wholly avoidable.

lines.x: Chairman Edelweiss! I'm glad you're here!
What is it? Slow down.
I-It's a letter by the Duke of Caster. The contents have already been disseminated to the public.
It's all about Kjerag... Mostly Karlan Trade.
What did she write?
About how we're not acting in good faith, and Sir Enciodes—
Give it here.
Notify all members of the Board that we are to have an immediate meeting at HQ.
Tell everyone to remain calm.

lines.st: Enciodes's first offense, and the source of all this tragedy, was to seek benefit from Columbia, brute-forcing the carving-out of a great pit and the construction of an observation station atop Mount Karlan.
In the wake of it, Catastrophe descended on Kjerag. This much is undeniable.
Amidst the terror and disarray, he committed his second offense. The humanitarian aid sent by Victoria was instead pocketed by him in the name of negotiation.
To legitimize this shameless act, Enciodes went on the attack against Victoria, leveling baseless accusations and slander against Victorian tourists and expatriates. This was his third offense.

His ultimate act of toying with the heart of the public was also his fourth offense. He colluded with Ratatos to fake the Browntail matriarch's death, and through duplicity, lead Clan Browntail into hostilities with Victoria.

lines.x: '...Victoria also has proof that Ratatos is hidden within the Vine-Bead Court. Those citizens who doubt this simply need examine the premises, though his guilty heart has led him to station his private army there.'
This is... all a bit much.
Hey, when I was suspicious of Enciodes earlier, you guys shouted me down like I was a conspiracy theorist. But it all makes sense now, doesn't it?
Since when? The whole letter's just Victoria's say-so.
The fifth offense explained it, didn't it? Enciodes told Ratatos to kidnap that Victorian reporter and hid her in the Court. Can't be a lie with all those details, can it?
And the sixth offense says in order to control the Court, he arrested all the clerics who wouldn't obey him. That matches.
The Court invited us all up the mountain for blessings, but Degenbrecher had some men stop us.
What about the seventh, then? It's obvious that Victoria wanted to get a bigger slice of the pie, but Karlan Trade refused them.
You're a real idiot, you know that? It's proof that Enciodes has gone and betrayed Kjerag.
Victoria's made a fair point about Karlan Trade going against all their contracts, and now we're the ones stuck with the bill thanks to Enciodes's stupid decisions!
So you think we should do what Victoria says? They want Karlan Trade to stick with the old mining agreement, and put Enciodes on trial!
They said they'd do a joint trial with the Vine-Bead Court, it'll be fine. Enciodes would be lucky if he just got banished for his crimes.
The only crime he's done is letting you run your mouth in this rebuilt pub!
So he's been doing all the good, while it's always someone else doing the sinning? Wow, what a coincidence!
...I don't understand any of this, but I know it's always us who have to suffer for it.
C'mon man, trust in Sir Enciodes, he's helped me out before—
What the hell was that?
O-Outside...
...I heard a lot of troubling talk on the way here.
Please tell me you've been doing more than admiring the snow.
I shouldn't have asked.
Is everything that happened still as you expected?
...If I said yes, would you believe me?
I would.
Because we're not unprepared. During the recent growth window, Karlan Trade—
I am afraid it is you who is the optimistic one this time, Gnosis.
You know that, no matter how many plans and contingencies we make, things will inevitably exceed our expectations.
From the three clans, the Vine-Bead Court, and stretching out to all the people of Kjerag, Victoria's methods are plain to see.
As someone who always bets every chip he has, you were never afraid of risk.
Thus, all this is all I deserve.
Dark clouds cover the twilight sky. The steel behemoths of Victoria trample across the white snowfields.
Caster's fleet...
I've heard this fleet was the one that drove the devils out of Victoria during the Battle of Londinium.
After the battle concluded, Caster took the time to restore its flagship, so that her Glory may live on.
How many upon this great land can boast of the might to stand against this force?
...We chose to walk the more difficult road. It is time we meet the end of it.
Whew—
Why are all of Kjerag's mountains so hard to climb...
Just bear it a little longer, Elena. We need to avoid the Vine-Bead Court, and this is the only road that does so.
A Rift Cloudbeast clambers up onto Elena's shoulder out of nowhere before jumping back into Joanna's embrace, letting out a high-pitched cry.
Heh, it's laughing at you.
Laugh all you want... I've done more exercise today then the past twenty-something years I've been alive...
Enya looks at the crestfallen Elena, letting out a light chuckle as she turns the corner.
She sees a simple camp by the side of the road, not far from where they stand.
There are many clerics of the Vine-Bead Court warming themselves by the fire. Amidst the crowd, Enya picks out a familiar figure with but a glance.
Why'd you stop, Enya?
...Is that Elder Adso?
Who?
I didn't think... I would run into you here.
Because you should not be here, Saintess.
You know, Elder, when you're talking with the Saintess, you should be more—
I know, and yet I also know this is not the road she is meant to be on.
...What if it is to search for Kjeragandr?
All shall seek Her in their time of need.
So, what is it you need?
You instructed me to go on the Pilgrimage, so that I may atone for the sins of all Kjerag to Her. Thus, what I need is to find Her, and pray to Her for forgiveness.
I had wanted to get you out of the Vine-Bead Court at the time.
...And in doing so, the Court would not be 'hindered' by me.
I had hoped that while you were off on the Pilgrimage, we would be able to sway the Duke of Caster into handling Enciodes, and that when you returned, it would be to both parties worn and wary.
The Court would then take advantage of your return to declare a miracle, thus gaining true control.
But your brother declared war on the Court too soon, and I could not allow myself to fall into the hands of Caster or Enciodes, whether as their prisoner, or as their puppet.

I presume you being here means you are thinking of being like the Court clerics of old, going to Karlan to the ancient refuges in the legends, where you can wait for the moment your enemies are 'worn'.
As such, there is no need for you to continue with your meaningless circuit, Saintess. Follow us to seek refuge atop the mountains.
There will come a time that the people will beg on bended knee for you, and us, to appear.
The silent elders of the Court behind him stand up one after the other, forming a semicircle before the Saintess.
Joanna glances at Enya with unease, her hand subconsciously moving towards her crossbow, but Enya merely gives her a light shake of the head.
I can't stop. The next Catastrophe is nearly upon us.
What...?
The Saintess is right, Elder. You must have noticed the strange weather these past few days.
We've already found an important clue about the 'Heart of Karlan'. We have the opportunity to learn the exact reason why a Catastrophe came to strike Kjerag.
Maybe even... the truth behind Kjeragandr.
...It is meaningless.
Elder—
Because Kjeragandr does not exist.
...What did you say?!
When I left the Court, you told me quite the opposite.
'Kjeragandr' can exist within the plants, the stones, our words and deeds, but you will not find Her true form atop this great land.
She is not real, only existing within our imaginations.
Not even you can blaspheme against Her, Elder Adso!

...When I was young, my faith in Her was much the same as yours. My father and uncle sent me to the Vine-Bead Court as a child, forcing me to secretly aid Clan Browntail with their mission, yet all I wanted to do was to serve Her in all sincerity.

When the Great Elder tasked me with managing the Hall of Writings, I was giddy with glee. Every word to do with Her, whether they be fragmented pages or ridiculous essays, were studied by me over and over.
But the more you read, the more you realize that the so-called Kjeragandr is nothing more than a piece of 'scripture' assembled by mortal men.
Dare you deny it, Saintess? You too have seen what is said to be the original copy. It is no more than a few scant phrases, nothing like what we have today.
It's not true, is it, Enya...?
From marketplace chatter to historical records, even foreign legends from who-knows-where. Add cold gales and snowy peaks, and you have the 'miracles' of Kjeragandr.
I persisted in my inspection of everything, hoping to find even the tiniest shred of evidence 'unlike the rest'. But all I found were more and more archetypical tales.
I even found signs of adaptation left behind by previous Court elders in the margins of a few books.
...Why didn't you tell anyone?
And what would I say?
What is Kjerag to you, Saintess?
That is not a question any one child of Kjerag can answer.
Because Kjerag already has its answer. This is a nation of faith.
Enciodes has tried to undermine it, the Duke of Caster has tried to swallow it, but even they dare not defy Kjeragandr, at least not openly.
But if She doesn't exist, then who founded Kjerag?
...Our country was never one built atop Her flesh and bones. Rather, it was Her country that was built atop our flesh, our bones.
It was the ancestors of the people of Kjerag who, amidst these snow-capped mountains of despair, labored tortuously to create a place where they could survive.
People could never understand why fate decreed they should struggle in suffering, whilst others thrived in merriment.
And so, abandoned upon these hopeless rocks, they had to seek consolation elsewhere, so that they may continue to live.
From the cries for some almighty leader and the prayers for majesty and morality of every generation of Kjerag, 'Kjeragandr' was born.
From the moment of Her birth, She became the foundation of Kjerag. Because of their need for faith, generation after generation must repeat a lie.
Kjerag needed virtue and law, needed art and aesthetics, needed family and tradition. With 'perfect timing', She would appear by our sides, leaving behind a few revelatory words of wisdom.
From this came an unbreakable order, one that has lasted time immemorial.
So in your eyes, this lie must be maintained at any cost, so that Kjerag itself can be maintained.
Indeed.
Every nation has its own lie. Kjerag's is 'Kjeragandr'.
...I disagree.
A lie can bring temporary reprieve, but it cannot become the eternal law through which we live.
You believe it is able to maintain order, so you care not for the sincere longing of the people. You treat their feelings as a tool you can control.
When this longing tears through the veil you have constructed, the Vine-Bead Court and the faith it tends to shall be shattered beyond repair.
From that point of view, you are your hated Enciodes's greatest ally.
There is only one thing I wish to ask you. Do you believe Kjeragandr exists?
I... but...
She sifts through her mind, through all her memories of that head maid... but she has already forgotten. How she looked, vague wisps of emotion, all forgotten.
There is no memory, no person who can vouch for her, no-one who can offer her words of comfort. Her heart lies empty...
...I do.
You claim this is all a lie, Elder Adso, which is proof that there is a truth to correspond with this lie.
And so I believe in the passion and goodness preached by the scriptures. I believe Kjeragandr exists in Kjerag.
Besides, I have already found the path to Her.
If you must refute me, then come witness alongside me.
Your 'faith' is pointless, Saintess. Even greater disaster awaits Kjerag.
Enciodes's short-sightedness has already left her faith on the brink of collapse. This is our last chance to save her.
Ever since the Pilgrimage began, you have treated me as part of your conspiracy.
Because we have both dedicated our entire lives to the faith. It is impossible for you to not understand my devotion.
Then what is it you wish me to do?
At a suitable hour, return with me to the Vine-Bead Court to regain control.
We shall have to look at our specific circumstances to deal with the Duke of Caster, but Enciodes must be eliminated.
You are a kind soul, and cannot bear to harm your brother. I understand.
Enciodes must leave Kjerag. That is the most we can allow him.
This is pure coercion.
In return, I can take you to the place you seek.
When I was young, I had searched all of Karlan. Since you must face the truth, this will be the quickest way to do it.
...I accept.
Enya?
But on one condition. If we really do find proof of Kjeragandr, you shall renounce all your schemes regarding Enciodes.
...Very well.
'Kjeragandr' above, witness our vows.
The rumblings of some distant commotion disturb the scant snow atop the trees. What should be a silent street is filled with an ominous warmth.

This used to be a road at the foot of Mount Karlan that led to the Vine-Bead Court, but now, Victorian soldiers bar the path as the treads of their tanks crush its surface. A blatant, impassable barrier.
The loudspeakers atop the tanks play a message in clear Kjeragi—
'People of Kjerag, do not panic. In accordance with Victoria's policy regarding the current situation, we will ensure everyone's safety.'
'This operation will not disturb the everyday lives of residents. We simply suggest everyone stays at home in this weather.'
'We also have a warning for the criminals who have abducted Victorian citizens, and for those who associate with them: release the hostages immediately, or we will take immediate action!'
Say, mister, mind if I cross the road?
I need to head home to get the fire going. It's about dinnertime, and my wife's waiting for me...
This area's off-limits. You either take a detour, or you wait.
But... I'm just trying to get to the other side of the road.
If safety's your issue, go ahead and search me. I've just got food on me, nothing else.
Are you with the Vine-Bead Court?
No, I'm just a smith.
Then take a detour or wait.
I've crossed this road for decades now...
Don't cause trouble for them, mister, nothing good'll come of it!
Just listen to them and get out of there already!
I will not be repeating myself a third time.
The old man totters away. He turns his head to look back. Through the window of his workshop, his wife looks back at him.
He waves his hand, telling his wife to leave.
Kjeragandr above... I wish to hear of your wroth...
Invaders! How dare you! Get out, you hear me?! GET OUT!
'Victorians' this, 'Victorians' that... It's the word I hate hearing most these days.
...Didn't think there'd still be anyone brave enough to kidnap one at a time like this.
Don't you get it? It's obviously just some worthless excuse. Now that the Victorians are here, Kjerag is about to change...
Vollpfosten! You're all just sitting back to watch while going on with your pessimistic talk! You're more spineless than a starving burdenbeast! I'm going to show them the real Kjerag!
If you're going to make a ruckus, do it somewhere further away! Don't drag me into this!
Right, right, go to Karlan Trade. They've got guards, don't they?
You're all a bunch of cowards...
Cowards? I get it, you've got guts, and you don't mind getting your hands dirty, but do you have a family?
If this is the outcome of the negotiations, if the three clans, the Vine-Bead Court, Karlan Trade... if none of them are going to do anything, what can we do?
Kjeragandr above... Forgive me for my fear, my cowardice...
Anger, fear, unease... All sorts of emotions roil across the unarmed crowd, but they have no effect on the armed soldiers across from them.
Their voices are drowned by the roar of the engines.
Your Grace, our fleet has encircled the passes into Kjerag's mountains.
The vanguard force in Kjerag has also surrounded the Vine-Bead Court. Kjerag's own warriors are powerless to resist us.
The whereabouts of Kjerag's Saintess are currently unknown, but we shall ensure that the moment she appears, we will locate her.
The situation is still under control... so forgive me for speaking bluntly.
There is no need for Your Grace to attend the battle in person.
Do not be hasty, Phillipa.
Choose your words wisely. If I may correct you, Kjerag is not our enemy, but a valuable partner in cooperation.
But Karlan Trade still has not given an official response to our invitation to negotiate.
Haha... I can imagine.
Enciodes is a prideful man. One assumes that having to process this failure must be of inconceivable difficulty for him.
You must have heard some of the tales about him, Phillipa. By all calculations, he is your kin as well.
If he really is the talented genius the rumors paint him as, then why did he abandon the chance to stand by your side, deciding to bury himself in the mountains instead? I simply don't understand.
Like I said, he really is the prideful sort.
I shall give him a little more time... Six hours should do it.
If the government of Kjerag continues to refuse to accept our agreement, then things can only end in a rather regretful manner.
Understood.
There is still one trifling matter I am unsure is worth Your Grace's consideration.
The matriarch of Clan Browntail, who by all rights should be dead. We have discovered further traces of activity from her.
She is currently hidden within the Vine-Bead Court, under the protection of the Black Knight.
Hmm... Enciodes would not go to great lengths to hide someone unimportant at a time like this.
It is not worth mentioning... but I am not fond of variables.
Find her, and find what she is hiding.
As you command, Your Grace.
Understood. I shall carry out this assignment to a limited extent, then await further instructions.
All hands take note. Team 1, guard the front. Team 2, head to the north gate to lock it down. Teams 3 and 4, find high vantage points to watch the Court.
Be on guard. If you allow even a single soul out, you shall have to answer to Her Grace in person.
I have not received any orders to allow Victorians in, sir.
I understand. We shall wait here for a reply.
The chairman has informed me not to accept any invitations. If you—
No matter, I am happy to wait here patiently, just like how every child of Kjerag waits for bitter winter to pass.
Speaking of, how do you people prepare for winter? I am rather curious.
I fear you may not have the opportunity to learn for yourself.
I am not here representing myself. My own experiences are not important, Mr. Edelweiss.
I believe these terrible conditions call for giving everyone an equal chance to express themselves, don't you agree?
...Why are you here?
Rest easy, Chairman Edelweiss of Karlan Trade.
I have come here for one very simple task: to invite Mr. Silverash to the negotiating table on behalf of Her Grace.
A simple, peaceful conversation, all for protecting the bond between our two nations.
You have brazenly sent an army to surround Kjerag, aimed your cannons at civilians, and dispatched your troops to blockade our markets.
Are these actions meant to be part of this 'peaceful conversation'?
Mr. Edelweiss...
Her Grace simply wishes to let the true master of Kjerag know that a speeding warship cares not where the people atop its deck run. For most, their best choice is to hold the handrail tight.
Profound understanding can come over the passage of eons, or it can come in the blink of an eye... It all depends on the 'commands' you have received, sir.
Get to the point, Victorian. You shouldn't need me to teach you how to say what you're here to say.
I would much rather call it 'sending a request', Mr. Edelweiss. A request from Victoria is a rather rare sight these days.
We cordially invite Mr. Silverash to a spirited discussion with Her Grace about the future of Kjerag.
We shall ensure the meeting goes smoothly. Her Grace has also commanded that none be harmed.
Then we too have our own request: Victoria is to immediately cease this military invasion.
I regret to inform you that such a thing hinges on the results of Mr. Silverash and Her Grace's talks. I am unqualified to make such a promise in Her Grace's stead.
Right now, my only hope is that there shall be a place warm enough for me to spend the winter... A fleeting hope that it shall be Kjerag.

lines.st: How should you present yourself when you face Her
I have asked myself that countless times, until tears of remorse flowed down my face, for I could not find the answer
Just like how, as I waver in the cold wind, I cannot find anything
The Heart of Karlan is mere mystic rhetoric
A painting within a dream, a mire amidst the mountains
The Vine-Bead Court says that, when you are on the brink of death, you shall see Her face
I believe this is my last chance
Yet I am unable to prove anything to you
I see only my mother by the bonfire, my wife underneath the curtains
The life I should have
You have come to the end of this faithless follower's epitaph, one written by him, for him
His last request to you
Is to wipe away the snow upon this tomb

lines.x: A lonely grave stands by the road. Enya and Adso stop in front of it.
All I did was bury him.
He only had time to carve himself a marker before he died.
So the bones we saw along the way are all...
The bones of humans.
Only human beings would use value and meaning to paralyze themselves... To climb Karlan, to walk to Her side, to witness Her existence, they are willing to give their lives.
The Saintess's right hand brushes against the worn wooden marker, completing its master's last wish.
Cold wind sweeps past her fingertips, as if urging her to grab hold of something. She lifts her head towards the snow-capped peaks, as her ears catch the sound of a young woman laughing...
Elder, did you hear—
She swallows down her unfinished question. The laughter echoes between the mountains, then fades, until it becomes nothing more than memory.
I didn't hear anything.
Please proceed, Saintess.
Enya hesitates for a moment before nodding, continuing onwards towards the darkening horizon, towards the peaks laden with thick snow.
The wind picks up, tangling itself in the Saintess's clothes. It leaves behind snow stuck to her brow, like how it sticks to the withered trees she walks past.
You need to try harder, we can't keep up with Enya if I have to keep pulling you—
Hah... Hah... We're only... two steps behind... No need to stand so close...
...Oh, right.
Just now... they were talking to something... right?
Hmm... I think I saw it too.
But there's nothing here except snow and rocks... Whatever, doesn't matter.
Elena forces herself forward a few more steps. Behind her, the snow where she stepped is suffused with a deep blue glow.
At the same time, she has the faint sensation of someone watching her, though the only one by her side is Joanna, gritting her teeth as she pulls at Elena's arm.
The billowing wind has numbed her brain. It must have been her imagination. As the thought runs through her mind, Elena hears a sigh.
...You took it upon yourself to enter a gamble you are fated to lose.
Saintess.
This is...
Indeed.
The depths of the mountains... The location of the Heart of Karlan.
I did as you asked, Enya! I dragged her all the way here!
(Quietly) We're here, don't just stand there.
Huh...?
...Wait, this, no, it can't be!
Elena puts all her strength into shaking her head and patting her cheeks before taking out a bevy of instruments, waving them against the cave wall.

It is an ordinary cave, one that can be seen anywhere in Kjerag. Its only unusual property is its proximity to Mount Karlan's peak. Enya heads deeper within, her breathing picking up pace as she does.

The deepest part of the cave is only a hundred meters from the entrance, barely wide enough to fit five or six men. The rocks are covered in a thin layer of moss and lichen, and the air is permeated with the humid scent of fungi and soil.
You are not the first who wished to find Her, and neither was I.

My greatest wish during my youth was to witness Her true form, to prostrate myself before Her. And so, I checked every book in the Hall of Writings, read every line of every poem, all to find proof of Her existence.
Many before me have left behind records and analyses. One mentioned this place, the only cave atop Mount Karlan.
I followed the endless, rugged mountain trails before finally finding myself here.
The records say that the clerics of the Court once sought refuge in the depths of Karlan.
Just so.
Perhaps you still recall the story of the Saintess who roused Kjeragandr.

According to legend, when Kjeragandr sent divine punishment, the Vine-Bead Court chose a hundred girls in succession to ascend the mountain and pray. The last of the girls was the one who quelled Her fury.
But in truth, that final girl was the daughter of a clan leader, which incited the great clans to ally and lay siege to the Court.
During the massacre, the surviving clerics fled here, after which came the so-called 'miracle'. Peace returned to Kjerag.
And how would you explain this 'miracle'?
Look at Kjerag as it is now, and the answer shall be clear. Who has ever heard of a war that came to an end for no reason?
If even the murder of a girl can transform into a sublime 'miracle', so too can political compromise become the stuff of legend.
Murder... So that is how you see the actions of the Court of old.
Indeed. To use murder to seek comfort, to allow ignorance to spur on the heart. This is the bloody debt the Court has borne in the name of 'Kjeragandr'.
With no god to promise us eternity, our every step is on thin ice, with no room for inattention.
I have brought you here to learn the truth behind Kjeragandr, Saintess.
And now... I shall wait for you, no matter how long you struggle to process it.
Enya! Come over here, quick!
This pile of rocks at the deepest part of the cave doesn't seem natural. It's more like a door that someone built.
...The clerics of old made this place into a sanctuary. Finding things here is hardly strange.
In other words... Behind this 'door' is most likely the hiding place of the ancient clerics, and a place where countless secrets of Kjerag are hidden...
What is more likely is that behind that door, you will find absolutely nothing.
Then let us bear witness, Elder Adso.
Enya half-shuts her eyes as she walks past the old man droning on about history, slowly approaching the end of the cave.
She takes a deep breath, then presses her body against the cave wall, like a reunion hug after a long time away.
Soil sticks to her hands and cheeks, moss pokes out from under her hair. It is as if she were fast asleep atop the earth.
I... can feel it.
According to my readings, Enya, behind this super-thin rock wall, there seems to be a giant cavern.
But there's no other way in... I can blow this door open, if we have to.
Wait, this is holy ground—
Do it.
Wha—Enya?!
O Kjeragandr above...
Pardon the intrusion, it's all in the name of truth!
I am in position.
As Her Grace's latest instructions state, Ratatos's feigned death must have a reason. She is most likely carrying important information on her person, and is to be captured alive.
This is the front, the courtyard is empty. Prepare for direct contact.
Do not attract attention. End it quick.
The only thing to watch out for is the Black Knight, who is most likely still here.
But where is she?
The intruder looks about warily, holding his weapon tight.
Not good!
A black degenbrecher flies at unbelievable speed towards him. The intruder is sent flying by the incredible force, slamming against the walls.
Ah!
I heard... more fighting outside. What in the blazes is going on?
Quiet down!
The trickiest enemies for us to handle are outside right now. Degenbrecher's risking her life to protect us.
The only thing you can do right now is sit down, shut up and not be discovered.
I... I heard the broadcast say that I'm a hostage abducted by Kjerag? Why...
Heh... Your fellow Victorians got their weapons out and started up their tanks. They're not letting anything out of this place.
So are they here to rescue you, or are they here to keep people in the know locked up in here so they can get their other plans done? The answer is obvious.
You mean they're using my rescue as an excuse to invade Kjerag...?
It's what you said... and it seems reality agrees with you.
...How did it all come to this?
If you've got a way to get the information out, you can ask your fellow countrymen that question.
When I came to this place, I thought I could help the oppressed people. With my camera and my words, I would be able to let more people know about the true Kjerag.
I believed all that I did had meaning...

...Should I be thanking you for your kindness or something?
Alright, alright, I'll treat you as an actual kind-hearted lady then.
So don't play dumb with me now. Why do you think what you've seen is the 'true Kjerag'?
And who do you think is doing the oppressing here?
Melting snow atop the roof becomes water dripping into the courtyard, causing ripples in the intruder's pooling blood.
Before him, the face shrouded in light looks right back at him.
Within the great hall, the candle flames are like chained bloodthirst, raging and roiling.
Such power... No wonder the reports on Kjerag emphasized the Black Knight's presence.
I knew you people would try to sneak in.
You... Hmph, to tell you the truth, I can't tell which of you is which underneath those masks.
That colleague of yours who came to Kjerag before, surely he told you this land is not one you can trample as you please?
Indeed. Your fame is known to us.
We made sure to make ample preparations.
So you intend to win through numbers.
None of us could hope to claim victory over you. It is only a mission, nothing more.
Your clothes may mark you as elites.
But here's something you wouldn't know without feeling some pain—
If you wish to pass, it will only be as a corpse.
Come.
It's rare that I have the chance to go all-out.
...I suppose I should consider you all willing to die.
She takes up a killing stance as the air is filled with the muffled cries of unsheathed weapons.
With drawn blades and cold gleams, they slowly step forward, stepping onto the puddles reflecting a muddled sky.
Upon this holy ground, a savage melee is about to begin.
The humid air warms and dries. Falling snow melts. Steam rises. Droplets splash about.
From the clash of steel against flesh come sparks of scarlet.
Hah—
Not bad! Again!
...I never thought it would be like this.
Should I not have come to Kjerag, Madame Ratatos?
That's a question you need to answer yourself, along with your country.
On the Kjerag side of things, I might have also considered using one as pure-hearted as you, so I can't really blame Victoria for doing it.
...So what exactly does the information I have mean to Victoria, and to Kjerag?
They're just making a fuss out of you to make it look like the facts are already settled. Don't take it too seriously.
We're hostages, nothing more.
I feel like this is all rather absurd.
Well, that's one way to sum it up. If your camera still works, this would make some excellent material for you.
If... I walked out that door, the rest of you would be fine, right?
If you did, they'd point their swords at you and confiscate all your material. You yourself might even disappear without a trace, with nobody else knowing what had happened to you.
With things having come to a head... You're the one who's meant to understand them the most, not me.
So are we supposed to sit here and do nothing?
I don't think the Victorians want to lose you, Miss Hostage.
Just calm down, wait, and then...
I don't care what your plan is, Enciodes, it's time to put it into action.
Reporting in!
We've built some temporary fortifications, just like you ordered, Sir Arctosz.
The enemy fleet hasn't fired yet. Instead, they're marching towards a place three clicks from us. How should we respond?
Our explosives reserves?
We've moved over all we could. We'll be risking our lives, but at the very least, we can delay the Victorian advance for a while.
Good.
Go get the troops in formation! Meanwhile, I'll have a chat with these Victorians!
The ship stops in front of him, its dark gray armor plating reflecting the sun's light. This is one of the rare moments in Arctosz's life he is forced to look up.
Who's your commander? Tell him to come out for a chat!
My commanding officer does not wish to come to blows with you, Arctosz Paleroche.
That all depends on why you've come to Kjerag. Don't tell me you're all just lost in the woods.
Her Grace has already made everything clear in her open letter. We must defend Victoria's interests while helping Kjerag stabilize following the Catastrophe.
All we ask is for the mastermind behind Kjerag's troubles, Enciodes Silverash, to receive the trial he deserves, which I believe is in line with Clan Paleroche's thinking.
The only thing I'm thinking is this: if you take even one more step forward, you'll pay the full price for it.
The only way you'll be going any further into Kjerag is over my dead body!
Back already? What're the commander's orders?
No changes. We keep guarding this final checkpoint. Once all their people are in, they'll definitely find Kjerag's arsenal.
They've been searching for ages now, but they still haven't found anything?
Yep. Even if Kjerag can't make any big moves like they said, I'm not willing to bet that that Enciodes guy doesn't have something up his sleeve.
Maybe—Hmm? Another transport truck.
Well, this one's all yours, buddy. I'm all tired out from checking all those beans last truck.
Fine.
The guard waves down the truck slowly approaching him. It obediently comes to a halt.
Sorry, mister, but as per Her Grace's orders, all vehicles passing this point are to be searched.
Unload your cargo—
A thick baton smacks into his head with a 'bonk'. Before the other guard can cry out for help, a shadow appears behind him, tightening itself around his neck.
We've cleared the road again, sir, just like the plan said.
Update Sir Gnosis on the situation.
Get those supplies transported. We can't keep him waiting.
She is unsure whose blood is blurring her sight as it runs down her cheek into the corner of her mouth.
She tastes an iron tang she has not tasted in a while.
Her twin degenbrechers quiver as they fend off blades coming at her from all directions. While her enemies' wily tricks have left wounds all over her body, she has not taken a single step back.
Seven or eight of her foes lie slumped on the floor around her.
Pah...
*cough*...
You are at your limit, Black Knight.
In truth, you can still choose to lay down your arms and surrender.
You're all mouth.
Lock up your silver tongues and come at me if you're brave enough.
Even you must bleed.
Would you not regret dying here, Knight?
...Hmph.
Meaningless drivel when said to someone—
Who holds their own life cheap.
Clang—!
She takes advantage of her foe's moment of complacency, sending him flying.
Most times, she could not let out a blow this strong. But now, her strength is fueled by a rage that had not burned in a very long time.
The mere sight of the scar-laden knight's furious form is enough to strike fear into the hearts of her enemies.
Some of them take half a step back.
What're you so afraid of?
The knight takes a shaky step forward, leaving behind two trails of blood upon the ground.
Her eyes shine brighter than ever, but it is the shine of bloodshot eyes. Her body is fast losing its ability to stand, yet her unwavering fervor still flows. From where, she does not know.
The candles of the hall slowly extinguish. The reflections on the floor become all the more blurry.
In the end, she staggers...
Several smoke grenades fly into the courtyard, covering everything in a cloud of fumes.
Who's there?!
They aren't Court guards. Someone our intelligence didn't account for is intervening. We can't continue the mission.
Abort mission, retreat with the wounded.
Made it right on time...
Victoria's elite hooligans won't be sorted that easily. We need to find somewhere safe.
Wake up. This is no time for a nap.
*cough* You're...
Sharp?
Take it easy with the talking, you're wounded.
Did the Doctor send you?
SilverAsh has been in contact with the Doctor the whole time. After hearing about the Catastrophe in Kjerag, the Doctor figured you'd run into trouble.
While we do have our hands pretty full at the moment, the Doctor did promise that if you ever found yourselves in a pinch, we wouldn't just stand back and watch.
I've already called Rhodes Island for reinforcements, they should be here any minute now.
What about Enciodes?
No need to worry. I believe SilverAsh has managed to take the reins of this situation.
Just hang in there, Degenbrecher. You're through the hardest part now.
...Are we able to maintain our current output?
Yes sir, ammunition stocks will be as expected. But, Chairman Edelweiss...
I can't help but doubt things. If what we're facing is the Duke of Caster's fleet, then even if we raised our efficiency tenfold, we still wouldn't be able to compete with them.
No need to worry. Just follow the plan.
We'll handle the rest.
I've worked for Karlan Trade for eleven years now, Chairman. I know what it's brought to Kjerag.
I believe in the both of you.
It seems you're ready now.
I suppose you don't need me for any further reminders, Master of Clan Silverash.
Since this is to be a negotiation, I thought I would change into something more formal.
Caster's army has laid siege to Kjerag, while her advance forces have control over several of our streets, forcing the civilians back into their homes.
Arctosz has brought his men to confront the Victorians, but they are a frail force in comparison.
The Duke's envoy came by Karlan Trade, asking me to pass you an 'invitation' to the negotiating table.
She is waiting for you, Enciodes.
It sounds like the situation is still within our expectations.
Look at you, admiring this lonely battleship... Does it not seem too weak as it is now?
Lately, I have found myself often thinking about a day from many years ago. The day my parents received an invitation from Clan Browntail.
My father told me that the reason why the Browntails and the Paleroches did not support us was because they had not seen what he had seen.
'If someone can take a step forward, even if it is only a single step...'
Even if it is the infamous Luca Browntail.
They knew of the worst-case scenario, but they did not hesitate.
Now I am at the age at which they passed away. Looking back, it seems that some things haven't changed.
But at least now I know why they did not hesitate.
In the wake of their passing, I followed my clan as we left Kjerag to head to Victoria.
It was a rough start. I was learning almost out of habit as I got used to the disdainful looks of my Victorian classmates.
The only thing that kept me going was the knowledge that one day, I would return to Kjerag.
But in the end, you went to Victoria too.
I fear I shall never forget the look on your face when I first greeted you at school.
...I was simply surprised by the way you greeted me, asking me if I still remembered our childhood promise like some sort of naive fool.
'Do you still want to make Kjerag big enough for the both of us?'
But it was also what convinced me that everything you said, the future you envisioned, was no mere fantasy.
Thus, I hitched my rope to Karlan Trade, which leads me to today.
Today is far from enough, Gnosis.
I am about to meet with the house, the most ruthless of creditors...
...No matter if I am there to see it or not, I will give Kjerag a tomorrow.
Finally, I want you to know that it's not just us supporting you. Many other people of Kjerag do too.
Karlan Trade's years of toil were not for nothing.
If you were to ask me what I had wished to hear at this very moment...
You always were able to get people to tell you exactly that.
Do you have an example?
Like when you told Degenbrecher you would treat her as nothing more than a pawn, and that when her contract expired, it was up to her if she stayed in Kjerag.
But you already knew her answer.
Perhaps why I wish to hear such words is precisely because I am not so sure you would give me such answers.
Hah, are you afraid?
When I declared I would make Kjerag into a grand stage big enough for us more than twenty years ago, I had signed away all rights to be afraid.
You have experienced it for yourself. I am sure you have not forgotten.
...But at times, I too need a few words of encouragement.
So, have Degenbrecher and I managed to provide you with them?
...Without a doubt.
I am truly glad... to have you both.
Perhaps, in the Kjerag to come, we will no longer need to make these usurious deals, or rely on the Vine-Bead Court to have faith.
Perhaps, you two will have the better of me in—
Enough with the self-flattery, Enciodes.
Go and see the Kjerag of your dreams for yourself.
If you are a coward who dares not face his own dream, then so be it. Degenbrecher and I have our own things to do.
Like right now. I should go.
As you wish.
The rock wall collapses with the first blast, revealing a deep cavern to the spelunkers.
Just like I thought. Looks like you really can go deeper.
Can't see much further than the tunnel entrance... We'll need to go down if we want to explore.
Enya?
Her question echoes through the cave. The Saintess does not answer, for she is watching the babbling old man purse his lips tight.
'Seek not my temple, but my heart.'
...I await you, Elder Adso.
His legs are like wizened tree roots, his dried-out throat like a shallow pool in the dry season.
There he stands, unmoving.
...I shall stay here and keep watch, while you enter to do what you must do.
But didn't you make a vow with Enya that you'd prove whatever's inside isn't Kjeragandr?
If you don't come with us—
I have already made myself clear. I shall keep watch outside.
If She truly does exist, then She should come out Herself to greet me.
Elder...
It seems as though, rather than learning the truth, you would much rather be blind to evidence of Her.
...I... no, I would not... I would never...
There are no answers here...
Then why did you bring us here, yet refuse to bear witness?
No, She does not exist.
Daughter of Silverash, and you, and you too, do not pretend you have not seen.

The hunters who tan furs with frozen fingers, the gleaners who choke upon dust, and the Oripathy-haunted with their torn skin.
Have you not seen them, they who kneel upon the dirt until their knees bleed, all to beg and plead to Kjeragandr for salvation...
No, to end their miserable lives early, even...
While you huddled together in your warm houses making merry, it was I, and the Vine-Bead Court, who would go where you were unwilling to even look at, to the homes of the impoverished and destitute.
It was I who soothed them, I who kept preaching to them that 'none shall be abandoned'... 'Even if you were born into a lifetime of suffering, there is still a god who loves you'...
It was we who built the nation where people can greet their ends with a smile, but you, but Kjeragandr—
This arbitrary liege lord, this king who sees all as Her cattle! She can, should, save these people, save all of Kjerag.
Yet She is aloof, magicking up some 'miracle' every once in a while to show her power...
Why is it only ever barely a handful of people saved?!
Now, you even wish to go see this monster, this tyrant, to ask Her to deny me, to deny the order we have so painstakingly built.
I... I cannot—
What more is left that could support this old man? He collapses into the dirt, crying out in anguish, as if his frail body were struck by some invisible hammer.
...Speak, tell me.
Why does She not know? Why does She not understand?
Why do you stay silent...?
Um...
Great Elder... Why is he so upset?
Elder Adso... I understand where you are coming from.
We are not allowed to witness the true face of God. Only through Her admonishments, Her creations can we describe Her.
But I still wish to believe...

That no matter what I see at the other end of this cave, my faith shall not be swayed. The virtues and commandments, the knowledge and wisdom She has conferred upon us. They have meaning.
I merely wish to see clearly...
what road the children of Kjerag have followed.
Enya turns and walks into the deep cavern without the slightest hesitation, towards where her faith lies.
...How long has it been now?
It has been five hours and fifty-seven minutes since we sent our ultimatum, Your Grace.
Hmm...
I must say, I did not expect Enciodes to choose to respond this way.
'Tis a pity things had to end like this.
Relay the order, all men are to—
That...
Your Grace! We've sighted one of Kjerag's high-speed battleships!
You don't mean... How many?
Just... the one, Your Grace.
Even without the aid of binoculars, those standing atop the deck can see it.
In the distance, a battleship slowly emerges from a snow-draped valley, its hue that of silver ash.
Perhaps everyone is thinking the same thing at this moment: This ship, no matter if you compare it to the fleet it is facing, or the mountains behind it, is rather on the small side...
Silverash...?
Your Grace, the ship is hailing us on a public frequency.
